anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Dzogchen or Mahamudra luminous selfless awareness, plus Pseudo-Dionysius's claim that the divine Cause is beyond both affirmation and negation.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence and pushes the idea away from candidate discovery toward extended synthesis.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: Luminous selfless awareness can look neither ownerless in a simple Bahiya sense, nor owned by an Atman, nor addressed to a devotional recipient. Dionysius also makes the Christian recipient unstable, because the final move refuses both affirmation and denial of God as a graspable object of intent.

claim_units: The practical movement is loosening attention from objects and ordinary self-story.
Udana 1.10 makes the sense fields sufficient and denies any locatable you there.
Brihadaranyaka Upanishad 3.7.23 makes the unseen seer the sole knower and inner Self.
The Cloud of Unknowing directs thought and will away from creatures toward God through naked intent.
The model predicts that traditions train a post-negation custody rule, not only a doctrine of self or no-self.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Programs with similar decentering exercises should diverge in outcomes and language according to the post-decentering recipient they train.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: De-identification practices loosen ordinary ownership of thoughts, then assign the released stance to no-self mindfulness, observing self, values, therapist relationship, or a higher power.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If de-identification outcomes and reports are unaffected by ownership or recipient framing, the custody structure is probably only a theological gloss.

exact_claim: After object and self-story loosen, traditions differ by where the freed attention is allowed to belong: no owner, witnessing Self, or God.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Interview transcripts should cluster around no one there, witnessing Self, and God or beloved as recipient, even when sensory content and quietness descriptions overlap.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Practitioners trained in Bahiya-style sense instruction, Advaita self-inquiry, and Christian apophatic prayer should diverge most in ownership and recipient language at the moment ordinary self-reference drops.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Micro-phenomenological interviews with trained Theravada, Advaita, and apophatic practitioners.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Reports cluster only by phenomenological texture, emotional tone, or teacher charisma, with no stable ownership or recipient pattern.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: A practitioner given no-location language, witness language, or God-directed language after similar objectless calm should later describe different belonging or authorization, while reporting similar quiet or contentlessness.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Changing the instruction at the handoff point should change later interpretation more than immediate perceptual content.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Controlled meditation instruction study with post-session interview coding.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: The initial phenomenology changes before doctrinal instruction, or the ownership language remains random after instruction.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Texts should pair negation of objects or self-story with instructions such as do not locate a self, know the unseen seer, or lift the heart to God.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Primary manuals should contain explicit or implicit custody rules near passages that loosen ordinary selfing.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Close-reading corpus of Theravada, Advaita, Christian apophatic, Dzogchen, and Mahamudra manuals.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: The relevant manuals loosen objects without any subsequent owner, Self, or recipient language, or use all three interchangeably.

primary_pair: Udana 1.10, Bahiya Sutta, Thanissaro translation, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/KN/Ud/ud1_10.html
Brihadaranyaka Upanishad 3.7.23, https://www.brhat.in/openlibrary/special/brihadaranyaka-upanishad/3-7-23
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The term attention imports modern psychology into texts that speak about clinging, Self, inner ruler, will, love, and God. Bahiya may be about ending clinging rather than leaving attention unowned. Brihadaranyaka may be ontological rather than attentional. The Cloud may intensify desire rather than suspend ownership.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: I did not find the exact phrase or structure of attention custody across Buddhist no-self, Advaita witness, and Christian apophatic prayer. However, close prior work already distinguishes common mystical experience, culturally shaped interpretation, monistic versus theistic typologies, witness-consciousness, and pure awareness. The originality is therefore a reframing of a known comparison around practice-level ownership language, not a standalone discovery.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: The primary contrast is real but narrower than the proposed model. Udana 1.10 uses the same seeing, hearing, sensing, cognizing sequence and then removes locatable self-reference. Brihadaranyaka 3.7.23 uses seeing, hearing, perceiving, knowing to identify the sole inner ruler and Self. This supports a strong no-owner versus hidden-seer contrast. The Christian leg is less exact: The Cloud of Unknowing chapter 3 begins with love and intent toward God, rather than first freeing neutral attention and then assigning it. Pseudo-Dionysius further strains the model because Mystical Theology chapter 5 places the Cause beyond both affirmation and negation, so even God as a stable recipient is apophatically unstable. Cloud source: https://sacred-texts.com/chr/cou/cou08.htm. Dionysius source: https://hekhal.org/texts/mystical-theology-dionysius/

title: The Custody of Unclaimed Attention
unlike_statement: Unlike common-core or constructivist accounts, this idea says the decisive variable is not only whether an experience is the same or differently interpreted, but whether training assigns loosened attention to no owner, the witnessing Self, or God.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Validated-then-abandoned hybrids, especially MN 22's raft, Heart Sutra reliance on Prajnaparamita after no-attainment, and Rinzai koan curriculum after kensho.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Substantially lowers confidence in the three-box typology. The contribution survives better as a two-axis or staged model.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: These cases mix confirmation, cancellation, and dissolution across stages. The raft must be grasped properly before being released. The Heart Sutra negates attainment but retains reliance and mantra. Koan practice may exhaust conceptual striving yet becomes a formal curriculum with tests. If hybrids are normal, the three types are not stable categories.

claim_units: A method's relation to its own result may be a comparative variable upstream of doctrinal content.
Mandukya is classified as self-confirming because analysis of waking, dreaming, and deep sleep culminates in turiya as Atman to be known.
Nagarjuna, Heart Sutra, Zen koan, and Eckhart are classified as self-canceling because their tools undermine graspable thesis, wisdom, attainment, or desire.
Zhuangzi, Dzogchen, and Sufi fana are classified as self-dissolving because the practitioner or frame of method becomes irrelevant.
The type should predict verification architecture, teaching strategy, and ethical consequences.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Methods that cancel their own frame should rely more on supervision, case judgment, paradox, and indirect signs than on checklist fidelity. Methods that confirm themselves should tolerate manuals and outcome criteria better. Methods that dissolve into embodiment should prioritize apprenticeship and exemplars.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Some methods confirm themselves by producing stable results, some cancel themselves by dissolving the problem frame, and some dissolve into skilled embodiment where the practitioner no longer follows the method explicitly.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If confirmation, cancellation, and dissolution do not predict training, verification, or institutional form outside contemplative traditions, the proposed structure may be domain-specific rhetoric.

exact_claim: At completion, contemplative methods face a self-authority question: some confirm themselves, some cancel themselves, and some dissolve the practitioner beyond method-talk, with consequences for verification and transmission.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Eckhart and Nagarjuna should share more indirect, anti-possessive verification patterns with each other than Eckhart shares with more kataphatic Christian mystics. Advaita witness practice should use more direct recognition language than Madhyamaka critique.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Method-type should predict verification architecture better than tradition membership.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Coded comparison of practice manuals, teacher testing language, and post-realization instructions across at least six traditions.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Christian sources cluster together by tradition more strongly than by method-type, or all traditions use the same teacher, lineage, and conduct criteria regardless of method relation.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: After Advaita inquiry, practitioners describe recognition as stable witness or Self. After Heart Sutra or Madhyamaka practice, they resist treating any insight as an attained object. After Dzogchen or fana language, they emphasize effortless presence or transformed embodiment over method success.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should report different relations to their own insight when switching method-types.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Structured interviews with practitioners trained in more than one contemplative lineage.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners report no stable difference, or differences track teacher personality and institutional context more than method-type.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Texts should mark when to use, trust, undermine, release, or embody a method. Examples would include raft language, preliminary versus main practice, koan curriculum before and after kensho, or thorn-removing-thorn metaphors.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Hybrid traditions should show explicit boundary management between method stages.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Historical and textual analysis of Zen, Dzogchen, Advaita, Madhyamaka, Sufi, and Christian apophatic training sequences.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: No boundary markers appear, or methods freely confirm, cancel, and dissolve without any pedagogical distinction.

primary_pair: Mandukya Upanishad verse 7, Sanskrit and translation, https://texts.wara.in/upanishads/mukhya/mandukya/ and https://gita.pub/upanishad/mandukya/verse/7/
Heart Sutra, Sanskrit and English, https://sa.wikisource.org/wiki/प्रज्ञापारमिताहृदय and https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Translation:Shorter_Prajñāpāramitā_Hṛdaya_Sūtra
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model imposes a modern reflexive category, method's relation to itself, on traditions that may speak instead of upaya, realization, grace, direct recognition, non-grasping, or annihilation of ego. It also risks treating stage-specific pedagogies as stable types.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: I did not find an exact three-type typology of contemplative methods as confirming, canceling, and dissolving at completion. But the self-canceling portion has very strong prior art in Nagarjuna, Wittgenstein ladder readings, Buddhist raft discussions, and Sells on apophatic unsaying. The dissolving portion is also standard in fana, zuowang, and Dzogchen descriptions. The new part is the comparative axis and its predicted consequences, not the individual patterns.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: The Mandukya and Heart Sutra contrast is promising but not clean. Mandukya verse 7 negates ordinary consciousness categories, then ends by saying this is Atman and is to be known, which supports a confirming limit move. Yet Shankara's commentary also says affirmative and negative language fail, so Mandukya contains a self-canceling linguistic element. The Heart Sutra negates aggregates, sense bases, ignorance, the four truths, wisdom, and attainment, which supports the self-canceling reading. Yet it immediately says bodhisattvas rely on Prajnaparamita and gives a mantra, so the method is retained as reliance and recitation, not simply canceled. The close read supports a two-axis model better than a pure three-box typology.

title: The Method's Reckoning: What a Practice Does With Its Own Authority at Completion
unlike_statement: Unlike Smith on Nagarjuna and Wittgenstein, Sells on apophatic unsaying, or the raft and ladder motifs, this idea proposes a three-way comparative axis and claims that the axis predicts verification and transmission better than doctrine alone.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Tibetan clear-light sleep and contemporary objectless-awareness sleep reports that claim awareness during the gap, not only after it.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence from candidate discovery to extended. The anomaly does not reject the model, but it makes post-gap grammar secondary rather than foundational in some cases.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If trained practitioners can give stable, carefully interviewed reports of awareness while the gap is unfolding, then the later report is not merely assigning evidence after an uninspectable silence. The model would need to distinguish in-gap phenomenology from post-gap evidential framing.

claim_units: Post-gap reports distribute authority instead of simply preserving memory.
Advaita deep-sleep arguments weight the subject or witness in reports such as I slept and knew nothing.
Early Buddhist cessation sources weight absence, transition, conditioned development, and post-emergence inclination rather than a surviving owner.
Daoist sitting-in-forgetfulness weights transformed aftereffect rather than hidden witness continuity.
Sufi dhikr or fana language may weight the Remembered or relation over the rememberer.
The distribution of evidential weight can be used as a cross-tradition comparison variable.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Anesthesia, blackout, trauma, and psychedelic integration reports
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Clinical and legal settings should show different admissibility grammars for blackout reports, anesthesia emergence, trauma memory, and psychedelic ego dissolution, and these grammars should predict what is treated as evidence of continuity, harm, or transformation.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: After an interval that cannot be inspected while it occurs, communities decide whether to trust subject continuity, absence of recall, physiological transition, aftereffects, or relational testimony.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If these domains use no stable admissibility patterns, or if all rely only on external measurement regardless of later report structure, the proposed structure may be limited to contemplative rhetoric.

exact_claim: After an objectless or silent state, the first later report is not neutral memory. It is a trained admissibility grammar that assigns evidential weight to the subject, absence, transition, aftereffect, or relation.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Advaita materials should privilege witness or subject continuity; early Buddhist cessation materials should privilege cessation, transition, and fruit; Daoist zuowang should privilege aftereffect and forgetting; dhikr or fana materials should privilege relation or the Remembered.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Traditions should show stable differences in which clause of a post-gap report they treat as admissible evidence.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind coding of primary texts, practice manuals, and practitioner interviews using subject, absence, transition, aftereffect, relation, teacher recognition, and behavioral fruit tags.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: If evidence weights vary mainly by translator, genre, or individual teacher rather than tradition and method, the model loses predictive value.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: The same practitioner speaks differently about sleep, cessation, or objectless meditation under Advaita, Theravada, and Tibetan prompts.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should shift evidential weighting when switching practice frames.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Structured interviews with dual-trained Advaita, Buddhist, Sufi, Daoist, and micro-phenomenology-trained practitioners.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners preserve the same evidential interpretation across frames, or report that the distinction is imposed by the interviewer.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Sanskrit, Pali, Chinese, Arabic, or Persian sources should mark subject, absence, transition, aftereffect, or relation through recurring lexical and grammatical patterns.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Original-language terms should support the proposed categories, not merely English pronoun artifacts.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Close read of Brihadaranyaka 4.3.23, Yoga Sutra 1.10, MN 44, AN 9.34, Zhuangzi zuowang passages, and primary dhikr or fana texts.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: The categories disappear or become unstable when checked against original terms and multiple translations.

next_loop_instructions: Close-read Thompson against Brihadaranyaka 4.3.23, Yoga Sutra 1.10, MN 44, and AN 9.34 before promoting the idea.
Build a coding rubric for post-gap reports with mutually exclusive and mixed tags: subject, absence, transition, aftereffect, relation, teacher recognition, behavioral fruit.
Test clear-light sleep and objectless-awareness reports as the anomaly class, using micro-phenomenological interview methods and serial awakening literature.
Check original languages and multiple translations before treating English first-person phrasing as evidence.
Ask practitioners whether the rubric changes cross-tradition interpretation or only renames familiar evidential disputes.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.71 to about 0.58. The cross-tradition admissibility rubric is useful, but the report grammar of dreamless sleep, memory, inference, witness, and post-hoc self attribution has close prior art.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say the distinction is already standard as pramana, memory versus inference, fruition review, teacher verification, or adab of remembrance, and that the new labels do not change practice.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can use the rubric to explain why cross-tradition disputes persist even when phenomenological reports sound similar.
questions: When you hear a report after deep sleep, cessation, forgetting, or remembrance, which part of the report counts as evidence?
Is this report-weighting obvious within your training, or does naming it change how the practice is understood?
Would your tradition reject another tradition's report because it weights the wrong clause?

primary_pair: Brihadaranyaka Upanishad 4.3.23 with Shankara commentary, https://vivekavani.com/bru4c3v23/
MN 44 Culavedalla Sutta plus AN 9.34, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/MN/MN44.html and https://suttas.github.io/an9.34
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model may make grammar carry what traditions assign to realization, teacher verification, metaphysics, ritual, or ethical fruit. Translation also matters, since first-person grammar in English may not map cleanly onto Sanskrit, Pali, Chinese, Arabic, or Persian terms.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: I did not find an exact source that classifies Advaita, Buddhist, Daoist, and Sufi post-gap reports by subject, absence, transition, aftereffect, and relation. But Evan Thompson's Open MIND essay gives a very close Advaita versus Nyaya analysis of the report I slept peacefully and I did not know anything, including memory, inference, minimal selfhood, and retrospective awareness. Alcaraz-Sanchez's micro-phenomenological study also directly studies reportability, pronoun use, and possible post-hoc self attribution in objectless sleep reports.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: Brihadaranyaka 4.3.23 treats apparent non-seeing in deep sleep as compatible with uninterrupted witness vision, because the object side has fallen away. MN 44 treats cessation through prior development, ceasing and arising fabrications, and post-emergence contact with emptiness, the signless, and the undirected. AN 9.34 makes the absence of feeling part of the reason cessation can be called pleasant. The contrast supports the proposed grammar, subject continuity versus conditioned transition and absence, but also shows that the grammar is already explicit in primary sources.

title: The Gap Report Has a Grammar
unlike_statement: Unlike Thompson on Advaita and Nyaya, or micro-phenomenology on objectless sleep reports, this idea says the evidential clause selected after a gap is a cross-tradition admissibility rule that can be coded across subject, absence, transition, aftereffect, and relation.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Other-power and direct-introduction traditions, such as Jodo Shinshu entrusting and Dzogchen pointing-out, appear to minimize self-powered agency while still functioning as paths.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence in the universal form of the claim. It supports a revised model where paths require some functional receiving capacity, but the amount and role vary sharply.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If a tradition can produce stable transformation through vow, grace, devotion, direct introduction, or teacher-field participation with very little reflexive self-management, then the minimum self may not be an architectural driver. The model would need a scale of required self-capacities rather than a universal requirement.

claim_units: Self-dissolving practice presupposes capacities for hearing instruction, effort, memory, error recognition, and self-observation.
SN 22.59 dissolves the person while preserving the causal process of liberation.
Chandogya 6.8.7 preserves the deepest Self while dissolving the reality of the student's apparent process.
Causal continuity, ontological identity, temporal collapse, and divine restoration are proposed as management strategies.
The management strategy should predict effort theory, verification, warnings, and post-practice fruit.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Therapies or pedagogies that deconstruct identity, such as trauma therapy, exposure therapy, psychedelic therapy, contemplative psychotherapy, or critical pedagogy, should have stronger screening, scaffolding, integration, and teacher-client safeguards than methods that merely add skills.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Any method that aims to dissolve or radically transform the learner's starting identity must preserve enough agency, memory, trust, and reflexivity for the method to work.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If deconstructive methods require no more scaffolding than ordinary skills training, or if safeguards do not track the degree of identity transformation, the structural claim is weakened.

exact_claim: A path that dissolves, denies, or transcends selfhood still requires a minimum functioning self, agency, memory, reflexivity, and continuity, and each tradition's strategy for managing that requirement predicts its practice architecture.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Manuals and teachers should warn against nihilism, dissociation, spiritual bypassing, false attainment, antinomianism, or ego-inflation in ways that match the path's self-management strategy.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The stronger a path's final self-dissolution claim, the more explicit its safeguards should be against premature or distorted self-loss.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Coded comparison of Theravada, Zen, Dzogchen, Advaita, Sufi, Christian apophatic, and Pure Land training materials.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Warnings show no relationship to self-teaching and track only institution, teacher personality, or general ethics.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Theravada stages, Sufi stations, and Advaita preparation should specify capacities, sequence, and error correction; sudden methods should shift burden to teacher recognition, pointing-out, vows, or post-recognition integration.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Long, effortful paths should develop more elaborate continuity mechanisms than sudden or other-power paths.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Comparative coding of practice sequences, teacher-student protocols, and attainment verification.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practice length and self-continuity machinery are unrelated, or sudden paths have equally elaborate minimum-self architecture before recognition.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Vipassana permits causal observation without ownership; Advaita permits witness or Self recognition; Soto Zen discourages gaining selfhood; Sufi practice permits surrendering agency into relation with God.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should report different kinds of permitted selfhood when switching methods.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Structured interviews with practitioners trained in two or more lineages.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners report no stable shift, or the shifts are better explained by temperament and teacher style.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Texts and practitioners should mark investigation of the investigator, hearing tat tvam asi, fana, kensho, pointing-out, or surrender as a crisis or verification point.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The point where a method consumes its prerequisite should be specially marked as difficult, risky, or decisive.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Primary-text close reading plus practitioner interviews and retreat-report coding.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: These moments are not specially marked, or transformation is described as ordinary continuity without any self-prerequisite tension.

primary_pair: SN 22.59 Anattalakkhana Sutta, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/SN/SN22_59.html
Chandogya Upanishad 6.8.7 through 6.16.3, https://shlokam.org/texts/Chandogya-6-8-7 and https://www.wisdomlib.org/hinduism/book/chandogya-upanishad-english/d/doc239333.html
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model may turn a researcher's philosophical tension into a practitioner's lived problem. It also risks forcing mixed traditions into clean boxes. Theravada, Zen, Advaita, Sufism, Dzogchen, and Christian grace traditions often combine effort, surrender, recognition, teacher relation, and ethical transformation across stages.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: I did not find an exact four-strategy cross-tradition typology that predicts architecture across Buddhism, Advaita, Zen, and Sufism. But the main pressure is heavily precedented in Buddhist personhood debates, Pudgalavada arguments, Collins on selfless persons, Engler on ego strength before no-self, functional-self accounts in Buddhist psychology, and contemporary self-pattern theories of meditation.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 22.59 requires monks who can listen, answer, inspect each aggregate, and become disenchanted, yet it denies that any aggregate, including consciousness, is fit to be self. Its closing grammar says the monks' minds were released, preserving process without owner. Chandogya repeatedly addresses Shvetaketu as a learner asking for more explanation, yet the repeated teaching identifies him with the Self of all this. This supports the proposed tension, but it is not new by itself. It is close to classical Buddhist debates over personhood and to Advaita pedagogy around recognizing the already present Self.

title: Every Path Needs What Its Teaching Dissolves
unlike_statement: Unlike Engler, Pudgalavada debates, Collins, or functional-self Buddhist psychology, this idea treats the minimum self as a comparative architecture variable across traditions and claims it predicts effort, verification, warnings, and fruit.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Convergence caused by direct borrowing, shared lineage, colonial translation conventions, English-language universalism, or dual-trained teachers rather than independent structural recurrence.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: This lowers confidence. The model needs an independence check, a translation-history check, and a practitioner-function check before convergence strain can be treated as research value.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: The model treats convergence as valuable when it reveals strain between traditions. But if the convergence is produced by transmission or shared modern vocabulary, the strain map may mostly record a history of borrowing or translation standardization. A second anomaly is practitioner equivalence: dual-trained practitioners may report that two instructions function identically in practice even when their doctrinal explanations diverge, which would make claim-unit strain less decisive than training context.

claim_units: Cross-tradition convergence should not be treated as direct evidence that two traditions assert or verify the same metaphysical claim.
The main research value of convergence lies in the translation strain exposed by attempts to align traditions.
Translation strain can be operationalized by identifying which smaller claim units must be bent, dropped, or added for an apparent match to hold.
A convergence remains valuable when it reveals a repeatable role in practice or experience.
Strong comparison should make incompatible metaphysical commitments more explicit rather than smoothing them away.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Legal and policy translation across jurisdictions
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Comparative-law analyses that score concepts by translation strain should better predict failed legal transplants, implementation disputes, and misleading harmonization than analyses that count shared terms or broad doctrinal resemblance.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Apparent convergence between legal concepts, such as privacy, due process, religious freedom, fiduciary duty, or public interest, should be evaluated by the institutional and procedural units that must be bent, dropped, or added for one system's term to function in another system.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If shared vocabulary and surface doctrinal similarity predict legal transplant success as well as or better than strain mapping, the structure weakens outside religious comparison.

exact_claim: Lumenary should treat cross-tradition convergence as a signal of translation strain: the research value lies not in saying two traditions prove the same claim, but in identifying which smaller claim units must be bent, dropped, or added for an apparent match to hold. A convergence is strongest when it reveals a repeatable role in practice or experience while making the incompatible metaphysical commitments more explicit.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: In cases such as Atman versus anatta, apophatic darkness versus Buddhist emptiness, or fana and baqa versus nondual realization, coders should identify recurring bends, drops, and additions in ontology, agency, final aim, authority, and verification.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: When apparent cross-tradition convergence is decomposed into claim units, high-quality comparisons should expose systematic incompatibilities that ordinary convergence scoring hides.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind coding of primary-text comparison sheets by scholars of Buddhism, Vedanta, Christian mysticism, and Sufism.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Independent expert coders cannot reliably identify the alleged strains, or simple convergence scores predict comparison quality just as well.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Some pairs should retain a shared practical role after decomposition, while other pairs should narrow sharply once claim units are separated.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: A convergence is strongest when role-similarity survives after metaphysical incompatibility is made explicit.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Apply a rubric to 12 to 20 narrow practice terms, with separate scores for independence, functional role, metaphysical compatibility, and practice-testability.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Role-similarity does not survive decomposition, or all cases collapse into either sameness or difference with no useful middle category.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: A Lumenary audit should downgrade some high-similarity pairs after showing that alignment requires deleting central commitments, while upgrading some low-verbal-similarity pairs because they share a repeatable practice role.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The method should prevent false syntheses that a convergence-weighting system would accept.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: A/B review of existing Lumenary convergence records, comparing old convergence scores with translation-strain outputs.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: The method never changes conclusions relative to ordinary expert comparison, or it only produces longer descriptions without decision impact.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Teachers should object to swaps such as anatta equals Atman, emptiness equals Godhead, or fana equals nondual awareness unless role, aim, and doctrinal frame are carefully qualified.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Practitioners should resist some interfaith equivalences even when scholars or popular writers treat the terms as convergent.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Structured interviews with dual-trained practitioners and lineage-specific teachers.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Experienced practitioners across traditions readily endorse the swaps without qualification and find no important practice or doctrinal distortion.

near_neighbors.difference: Smith does not present a Lumenary-style operational rubric of claim-unit bends, drops, and additions, nor does he tie the output to publication scoring.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The anti-sameness theory of comparison is already central prior art.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close at the level of comparative method. Smith attacks comparison that projects similarity into objective connection and stresses difference, invention, redescription, and rectification.
near_neighbors.source: Jonathan Z. Smith, In Comparison a Magic Dwells, https://classics.osu.edu/sites/classics.osu.edu/files/Magic_Dwells.pdf

near_neighbors.difference: Freiberger's model is broader and methodological. The Lumenary idea focuses specifically on convergence and asks what must deform for an apparent match to hold.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The proposed workflow looks like a specialized version of existing comparative-method theory.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close structurally. Freiberger treats comparison as a second-order method with selection, description, juxtaposition, redescription, rectification, and theory formation. This already makes comparison valuable as a controlled operation, not as simple evidential accumulation.
near_neighbors.source: Oliver Freiberger, Elements of a Comparative Methodology in the Study of Religion, Religions 9, no. 2, 2018, https://www.mdpi.com/2077-1444/9/2/38

near_neighbors.difference: Panikkar emphasizes functional equivalence and dialogue. The Lumenary model adds explicit tracking of losses, additions, and incompatible commitments.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the role-similarity without identity claim. Some novelty remains in the strain-audit mechanics.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Panikkar's homeomorphic equivalence compares concepts that play similar roles in different religious systems while respecting specificity and avoiding mistaken comparisons.
near_neighbors.source: Raimon Panikkar, homeomorphic equivalence glossary, https://www.raimon-panikkar.org/english/gloss-homeomorphic.html

near_neighbors.difference: Katz focuses on mystical experience and mediation by conceptual schemes. The Lumenary idea generalizes the caution into a broader comparative-research rubric.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for refusing to treat convergence as proof of sameness.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Katz's constructivist critique blocks the move from similar mystical reports to a shared, neutral experience or metaphysical truth.
near_neighbors.source: Steven T. Katz, Language, Epistemology, and Mysticism, discussed in Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy, https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/mysticism/

near_neighbors.difference: Sharf is more skeptical of the category of experience itself. The Lumenary model keeps convergence as useful, but relocates value into strain mapping and practice-role comparison.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the critique of experiential convergence as evidential weight.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Sharf criticizes the authority of experience language and the master narratives built around it, especially in Buddhist studies and comparative mysticism.
near_neighbors.source: Robert H. Sharf, Experience, from Critical Terms for Religious Studies, Cambridge page, https://www.cambridge.org/core/books/abs/religious-experience/robert-h-sharf-experience-from-critical-terms-for-religious-studies/F0A870226B557378A2B8F8B882878636

near_neighbors.difference: Clooney's method is theological, reader-transformative, and text-centered rather than an explicit scoring protocol for convergence quality.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The Lumenary proposal is a secularized and schematized comparative-theology habit.
near_neighbors.overlap: Moderate to close. Clooney's comparative theology uses close reading, micro-comparison, analogous texts, and difficult comparands that can disrupt settled discourse.
near_neighbors.source: Francis X. Clooney, Reading Religiously across Religious Borders: A Method for Comparative Study, Religions 2018, https://bpb-ap-se2.wpmucdn.com/thinkspace.csu.edu.au/dist/c/3891/files/2020/10/Clooney-on-Method.pdf

near_neighbors.difference: Sandmel mainly warns against abuse of parallels. The Lumenary idea gives convergence positive use as a generator of explicit distinctions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The negative caution is old, while the constructive strain rubric is newer.
near_neighbors.overlap: Moderate to close. Sandmel's warning against overdrawn parallels anticipates the idea that similarity should not be treated as evidence of source, derivation, or deep sameness without disciplined analysis.
near_neighbors.source: Samuel Sandmel, Parallelomania, Journal of Biblical Literature 81, 1962, citation and summary at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parallelomania

near_neighbors.difference: Translation studies is not specifically a comparative-spirituality originality audit, and it does not normally score metaphysical convergence claims.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The deformation vocabulary is inherited, but its use as a Lumenary audit tool may be useful.
near_neighbors.overlap: Moderate. Translation theory already studies shifts, equivalence, functional substitution, and the absence of exact correspondence between source and target systems.
near_neighbors.source: Translation studies on shifts, formal correspondence, dynamic equivalence, and untranslatability, for example Catford and Nida overviews, https://www.translationdirectory.com/articles/article2019.php and https://www.britannica.com/biography/Eugene-A-Nida

next_loop_instructions: Build a one-page translation-strain rubric with fields for claim unit, role, ontology, agency, practice aim, authority, verification, what is bent, what is dropped, what is added, and whether role-similarity survives.
Test the rubric on three narrow pairs: Atman versus anatta, apophatic darkness versus Buddhist emptiness, and fana or baqa versus nondual realization.
Add an independence field so convergence caused by borrowing, colonial translation, shared modern discourse, or teacher cross-training is not overvalued.
Ask comparative-religion scholars whether this is merely Smith, Katz, Sharf, Panikkar, Freiberger, Clooney, or Sandmel in workflow language.
Do not publish as a candidate discovery unless the rubric produces decisions that ordinary convergence scoring would not produce.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.78 to about 0.56. The idea is not a new comparative principle. Its best contribution is a useful synthesis and operationalization of established anti-perennialist, comparative-method, comparative-theological, homeomorphic-equivalence, and translation-shift approaches for Lumenary's claim-unit workflow.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners and scholars say this is standard comparative method, already captured by constructivism, homeomorphic equivalence, thick comparison, comparative theology, translation-shift analysis, or warnings against parallelomania.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: They say the model gives them a concrete, reusable way to prevent false equivalence while preserving genuine practice-role comparison, especially if it changes ratings, publication decisions, or teaching practice.
questions: When you hear a claim that two traditions converge, do you already ask which doctrinal or practice units must be dropped, bent, or added for the match to work?
Would a translation-strain sheet change how you compare terms such as anatta, Atman, emptiness, Godhead, fana, witness, soul, and awareness?
Can you name a case where a shared practice role survives even though the metaphysical explanation must remain incompatible?

primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: Moderate to high. The model may redescribe mature comparative-religion method in Lumenary language. It may also over-textualize living practices, since practitioners may compare training effects, ethical fruit, embodiment, and teacher-student transmission rather than decomposed claim units.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: No exact prior source was found using the same formula, convergence as translation strain, not evidence weight. However, the core components are heavily precedented in comparative method, anti-perennialist critiques of shared mystical experience, comparative theology, homeomorphic equivalence, translation studies, and warnings against parallelomania.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: A surface convergence around inward inquiry and liberation strains quickly. SN 22.59 examines form, feeling, perception, fabrications, and consciousness, then refuses each as self because each is inconstant, stressful, and not controllable as mine, self, or what I am. Chandogya 6 repeatedly identifies the student's deepest reality with the subtle essence, truth, and Self of all. To align them, at least four claim units must be bent or dropped: whether consciousness can be ultimate, whether liberation is disidentification from all aggregates or recognition of a deepest Self, whether final language is non-appropriation or identity, and whether the pedagogical subject is dissolved or revealed. The model works well on this pair because the apparent convergence becomes useful only after the incompatibilities are itemized.

primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: The pair supports only part of the model. Brihadaranyaka 3.7.23 repeatedly places an inner ruler within earth, beings, organs, mind, and knowledge, then identifies this as Self and immortal. SN 22.59 examines form, feeling, perception, fabrications, and consciousness, then rejects each as mine, self, or what I am because each is inconstant and not masterable. Both disturb ordinary identity and boundary-making, but their final authority structure diverges sharply: one licenses an unobjectifiable Self, the other de-authorizes ownership of all aggregates including consciousness.

primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. Advaita sources often say consciousness is immediately self-disclosing, not inferred. Buddhist sources may frame not-self as pragmatic release, not epistemic burden allocation.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Important near-neighbors include Advaita self-luminosity and pramana debates, Buddhist not-self strategy, Madhyamaka refusal of extremes, and modern philosophy of consciousness. The audit did not reach enough Sanskrit or Pali commentarial material to confirm the inference-policy claim historically.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: The inference-policy frame is plausible but partly anachronistic. Brihadaranyaka 2.4 commands seeing, hearing, reflecting on, and knowing the Self, then asks how the knower could be known when all is Self. This is not simply an inference from failed objectification. SN 22.95 compares consciousness to a magic trick and asks what substance could be found in it, but it functions as contemplative disenchantment rather than a formal burden-of-proof rule. The model is strongest if it is stated as an interpretive reconstruction of argument-behavior, not as the texts' explicit logic.

title: Residual Burden of Proof After Negation
unlike_statement: Unlike ordinary residue-policy language, this idea isolates the rule that decides whether a proposed remainder is allowed to count. Its originality depends on proving that such a rule is operative in texts or practice, not merely useful for modern analysis.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Nirodha samapatti and cessation reports, where practitioners report a discontinuity or absence of consciousness rather than a pressure to posit an enduring witness.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence from candidate-discovery to novel-synthesis.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If advanced practice can end reported consciousness without producing a subject-remainder pressure, the pressure is not an invariant. It may be tradition-specific, stage-specific, or retrospectively constructed.

claim_units: Self-negation can generate pressure to posit a final subject.
Traditions manage this pressure differently: ontologize, recursively analyze, bracket phenomenologically, or model it cognitively.
The pressure is separable from the inference policy used to interpret it.
Modern self-modeling and meta-awareness research can test the pressure without settling metaphysics.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Users should attribute a central agent more strongly when visible content and local mechanisms are made opaque but coherent behavior remains.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: When a model's outputs are stripped of content-level explanations, observers may feel pressure to posit a hidden agent, controller, or center behind behavior.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Opacity reduces agent attribution or produces only uncertainty, with no pressure toward hidden-subject explanations.

exact_claim: The bridge between Upanishadic witness language, early Buddhist not-self analysis, and modern consciousness research is remainder pressure: the felt or conceptual demand to posit a final subject after object-like contents have been negated.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Practitioners should describe a pull toward phrases such as something remains, knowing remains, no one owns it, or cannot locate the knower before choosing metaphysical language.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Remainder pressure should be detectable in reports before doctrinal interpretation is applied.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Micro-phenomenological interviews after self-inquiry, vipassana, open monitoring, and cessation-adjacent retreats.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Reports move directly into doctrine or simple absence without any intermediate pressure category.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Similar reports of content reduction should be resolved as Self, no-self, bracketed awareness, or self-model artifact depending on training context.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Training style should shape how the pressure is resolved, not whether the pressure can arise at all.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Longitudinal study of practitioners cross-trained in Advaita and Buddhist methods.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Training context predicts the entire phenomenology so strongly that no shared pressure stage can be identified.

primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model may psychologize metaphysical claims and may invent a universal pressure that not all practitioners experience.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The search found close components in minimal phenomenal experience, self-model theory, attention and agency factors, pure consciousness debates, and nirodha research. No exact prior phrase remainder pressure appeared, but the mechanisms are not new enough to call a discovery without empirical coding.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: The texts show divergent management of remainder, but they do not clearly show a felt pressure. Brihadaranyaka supplies a seer-knower through a cosmological and theological sequence. SN 22.59 applies a disciplined not-self template to aggregates. The pressure variable is therefore not directly textual. It is an interpretive bridge from these texts to practice reports and cognitive science.

title: Remainder Pressure as the Hidden Variable in Self-Negation
unlike_statement: Unlike MPE, pure consciousness, or self-model literature, this idea names the pressure to promote a residual subject as a variable distinct from the tradition's final interpretation of that pressure.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Advaita practitioners and texts often deny that the witness is an inference, while Buddhist practitioners may deny that objectless awareness is a shared datum at all.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers originality and confidence. The model remains useful as a comparison hypothesis, not as a discovery.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If Advaita recognition is claimed as immediate and Buddhist insight is theory-laden from the start, then the model's shared phenomenology plus divergent inference structure collapses. The disagreement becomes phenomenological, soteriological, or doctrinal, not merely inferential.

claim_units: Advaita and Buddhism may share a phenomenological ground in de-objectification practice.
The dispute concerns what objectless awareness authorizes, not only what exists.
Advaita fills the gap with a transcendental inference to Atman/Brahman.
Buddhism refuses the inference and treats filling the gap as renewed clinging or wrong view.
The translation strain lives in inferential policy more than phenomenology.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Debates over consciousness in artificial systems
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: AI-consciousness debates should split into policies analogous to Atman/anatta: infer a hidden subject from coherent knowing-like behavior, refuse the inference without direct evidence, or suspend both subject and no-subject claims.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: When behavior lacks an observable subject or inner object, interpreters choose policies about what absence of object permits them to infer about hidden subjectivity.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: The debate is explained entirely by functional performance, ethics, or biology, with no recurring inferential-policy split.

exact_claim: The Atman/anatta contradiction is best understood as a disagreement about inferential authority: what objectless awareness permits one to conclude about what persists when experience has no object.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: The same practitioner can describe a contentless or objectless episode neutrally, then explain how Advaita and Buddhist teachers authorize different conclusions from it.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should report similar de-objectified phenomenology but switch interpretive policies by practice frame.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Within-subject interviews with practitioners trained in both Advaita self-inquiry and Theravada or Madhyamaka-oriented practice.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners report that the experiences themselves differ from the start, or they cannot separate experience from doctrinal interpretation.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Advaita sources should use continuity, memory, or self-luminosity to defend awareness across apparent absence, while Buddhist sources should explain retrospective knowledge without positing a continuous knower.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Nirodha and deep sleep should become pressure tests for inferential policy.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Close reading of Mandukya/Gaudapada/Shankara, Pali cessation texts, and Laukkonen-style cessation interviews.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Either tradition has no stable explanation, or both use the same explanatory rule.

primary_pair: Mandukya Upanishad verse 7, https://www.shlokam.org/text/mandukya-upanishad.htm
SN 22.59 Anattalakkhana Sutta, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/SN/SN22_59.html
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The claim that both traditions share phenomenology but differ in inference may be too clean. Training may shape phenomenology itself, and Advaita often treats witness-consciousness as direct recognition rather than inference.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The idea cites strong sources, but the audit found close prior art in Codex residue-policy work, Advaita self-luminosity scholarship, Stace/Forman/Katz interpretation debates, Loy's Buddhism and Advaita comparison, and Madhyamaka self/no-self refusal. More work is needed in Sanskrit, Pali, Gaudapada, Shankara, Abhidhamma, Yogacara, and Madhyamaka commentaries.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: Mandukya 7 negates inward, outward, mixed, massed, cognitive, and non-cognitive descriptions, but still names the fourth as nondual Self to be realized. SN 22.59 does not discuss objectless awareness in Mandukya's terms. It examines each aggregate and rejects identification with consciousness because it is inconstant, stressful if clung to, and not masterable. The candidate's shared phenomenology is therefore not established by these primary texts. The inferential gap is a plausible comparative reconstruction, but it is not directly shown without practice reports or commentarial bridges.

title: The Inferential Gap: Atman and Anatta as Competing Policies for Objectless Awareness
unlike_statement: Unlike the prior Codex burden-of-proof idea, this version explicitly claims that objectless awareness underdetermines metaphysics and that Advaita and Buddhism differ primarily in inferential authority. That is a useful sharpening, but the main structure was already present.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Radical Jodo Shinshu other-power: true shinjin is not a human capacity at all, but Amida's working, and self-assessment as capacity may itself be self-power calculation.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers originality and confidence. The model survives only if capacity is redefined as functional support locus rather than practitioner-owned competence.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If entrusting is not merely low agency but a denial that the practitioner owns the decisive receiving condition, then the ledger misnames a theological claim as a psychological capacity. A similar Dzogchen objection says recognition is not a practitioner's skill but primordial awareness recognized through introduction.

claim_units: The needed practitioner support is not one generic minimum self, but a five-column ledger: receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration.
A path can reduce one capacity load only by raising another or relocating the load to a teacher, vow, community, text, or post-insight discipline.
Effortful Buddhist practice places high load on agency, memory, and reflexivity before insight.
Other-power practice lowers self-powered agency but raises or relocates receiving, hearing, entrusting, and freedom from calculation.
Direct-introduction practice lowers production at the threshold but raises recognition, teacher relation, and stabilization.
The model predicts distinct failure modes when a load is absent, inflated, or placed in the wrong locus.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Identity-transforming modalities such as trauma therapy, psychedelic therapy, exposure therapy, Internal Family Systems, and critical pedagogy should differ from ordinary skills training by having stronger trust-building, screening, memory supports, facilitator authority, and integration practices. Low-agency modalities should compensate with higher relational containment and post-session integration.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Any method that transforms identity must place functional load somewhere: client agency, trust in therapist or method, memory of practice, reflexive self-observation, group support, or integration after the decisive session.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Identity-transforming methods require no more scaffolding than ordinary skill acquisition, or failures do not track where agency, trust, memory, reflexivity, and integration are placed.

exact_claim: Awakening paths should be compared by where they place five functional loads: receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration. Effort, gift, direct introduction, and practice-realization do not remove capacity requirements, they redistribute them to the practitioner, teacher, vow, community, text, present act, or post-insight discipline.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Right-effort and gradual-path texts should emphasize intention, repetition, memory, correction, and ethical maintenance. Other-power and direct-introduction texts should not show zero safeguards, but should show safeguards around doubt, false trust, teacher qualification, distraction, antinomian misuse, recognition, and stabilization.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Practice manuals should show patterned safeguards matching their load distribution.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Coded comparison of SN 45.8, Shinran letters and Tannisho, Longchen Rabjam Trekcho materials, Dogen Bendowa, and contemporary lineage manuals.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Manuals show no relation between path type and safeguard profile, or safeguards track only institution, genre, or teacher personality.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: The same practitioner reports that vipassana authorizes effortful observation and memory of instructions, Pure Land authorizes relinquishing calculative self-power, Dzogchen authorizes recognition and non-distraction, and Soto practice-realization authorizes full enactment without future-gaining.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should report different permitted self-capacities when switching methods.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Structured interviews with practitioners trained in at least two of Theravada, Jodo Shinshu, Dzogchen or Mahamudra, Soto Zen, Advaita, and Sufism.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Reports are explained better by temperament, teacher style, or retreat setting, with no stable method-specific capacity shift.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Gift practice distorted into agency produces anxious self-checking or pride. Direct introduction without stabilization produces reification, distraction, or antinomian confidence. Effort practice without memory or reflexivity produces mechanical repetition or bypassing.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Failures should follow the misplaced load, not merely practice intensity.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Re-coding adverse meditation reports, teacher case notes, and practitioner interviews with ledger fields.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Failure types do not correlate with load placement, or all traditions show the same generic adverse patterns after controlling for trauma, isolation, sleep, and teacher support.

next_loop_instructions: Rename practitioner capacity as functional support locus when auditing Other Power, grace, and Dzogchen, so the model does not smuggle self-power into traditions that reject it.
Build the five-field ledger as an explicit coding sheet with values for practitioner-owned, teacher-held, vow-held, community-held, text-held, present-act-held, and post-insight-held load.
Code primary manuals before using broad tradition labels, beginning with SN 45.8, Tannisho, Shinran letters, Longchen Rabjam Trekcho, Bendowa, and one Advaita prerequisite text.
Ask Jodo Shinshu and Dzogchen practitioners whether receiving and recognition can be called capacities without doctrinal distortion.
Do not promote above extended unless coded manuals and practitioner interviews show predictive failure patterns that Engler, Upaya, and prior Lumenary work do not already explain.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.61 to about 0.49. The ledger is useful and testable, but it largely extends the audited Lumenary minimum-self thesis and known work on Engler, Upaya, Buddhist personhood, self-pattern theory, and adverse meditation contexts.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is just Engler, Upaya, adhikara, two truths, ordinary pedagogy, or Shinran's self-power versus Other Power distinction in new vocabulary. Pure Land or Dzogchen teachers say the model misdescribes their decisive moment by making it a human capacity.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can use the five fields to predict real differences in screening, warnings, teacher dependence, post-insight integration, and error patterns across traditions better than the simpler effort-versus-gift contrast.
questions: Is receiving or entrusting a practitioner capacity in your tradition, or is that already a self-power mistake?
Does a five-field ledger change how you would screen, sequence, warn, or verify students?
Are the proposed failure modes obvious from standard training, or does the ledger predict something you would not otherwise notice?

primary_pair: SN 45.8 Vibhangasutta, SuttaCentral, https://suttacentral.net/sn45.8/en/bodhi
Shinran, Letter to Senshin, Collected Works of Shinran, https://shinranworks.com/letters/a-collection-of-letters-zensho-text/what-we-call-other-power/
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model may smuggle self-power back into Shinran by treating entrusting as a practitioner capacity. It may also psychologize Dzogchen recognition as a personal skill rather than primordial awareness recognizing itself. The safer formulation is that paths require functional support loci, not necessarily capacities owned by the practitioner.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Search found very close prior art in Engler's ego-strength-before-no-self thesis, Buddhist personhood debates, Pudgalavada arguments about karma and liberation, Buddhist functional-self and self-pattern theory, Upaya as capacity-sensitive pedagogy, spiritual bypassing literature, and Lumenary's prior audited idea Every Path Needs What Its Teaching Dissolves. I did not find an exact five-column gift-effort ledger with relocation rules across Pure Land, Dzogchen, right effort, and practice-realization, but the main architecture was already specified in the prior Lumenary audit's next-loop instruction.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 45.8 strongly supports the agency side of the ledger: right effort requires desire, striving, energy, applied mind, and maintenance across wholesome and unwholesome states. Shinran's Letter to Senshin sharply resists that grammar by denying calculative working on the practicer's side and placing settlement in Other Power. The ledger is useful because it notices the contrast, but the phrase receiving capacity is not simply textual in Shinran. The text more nearly says that the decisive settling is not possessed by the practitioner. Longchen Rabjam's Trekcho instruction, https://www.lotsawahouse.org/tibetan-masters/longchen-rabjam/lama-yangtik-trekcho-instruction, supports the relocation pattern by rejecting contrived effort while still requiring recognition of what has been introduced, undistracted abiding, vows, devotion, renunciation, and post-meditation conduct.

title: The Capacity Ledger of Gift and Effort
unlike_statement: Unlike Engler, Upaya, Pudgalavada personhood debates, or the prior Lumenary minimum-self claim, this idea says the comparative unit is a five-field load distribution, and that effort, gift, direct introduction, and practice-realization can be tested by where they relocate receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration. Because this follows directly from an earlier Lumenary audit instruction, it is best treated as an extension.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Successful hybrids and dual-trained practitioners who switch methods smoothly without reported directional resistance.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence from candidate discovery to novel synthesis. The model is promising only if practitioner reports and manual coding show directional resistance beyond ordinary difficulty, teacher style, or conceptual disagreement.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If a vipassana-trained practitioner can move into Advaita self-inquiry, devotional surrender, or Dzogchen recognition without attention persistently tracking impermanence, non-ownership, or analytic deconstruction, then grain may be a comparative description rather than a lived capacity structure. Chan, Zen, modern cross-training, and eclectic contemplative therapy are especially important anomaly classes.

claim_units: Preparatory capacities are shaped by the dissolution they serve.
The same named capacity, such as attention or self-observation, functions differently in Theravada, Advaita, Sufism, Pure Land, Dzogchen, and Soto Zen.
Capacity and dissolution are mutually conditioned rather than independently first.
The five ledger variables carry direction, not only level.
When a practitioner trained in one tradition's grain attempts another tradition's dissolution, the old grain should resist or distort the new practice.
Adverse contemplative effects should cluster by grain mismatch, not only by missing prerequisites or practice intensity.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Clients and therapists crossing modalities should show grain-specific resistance: CBT-trained clients may convert psychoanalytic free association into problem solving, somatic-therapy clients may treat cognitive restructuring as disembodiment, and IFS-trained clients may over-personify processes in exposure work. Successful integrative therapy should include explicit re-graining, not only adding techniques.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: A therapeutic capacity such as attention to emotion is not neutral. CBT, psychoanalysis, Internal Family Systems, somatic therapy, and exposure therapy train attention toward different transformations, such as belief revision, transference interpretation, parts differentiation, nervous-system regulation, or fear extinction.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Therapeutic skills transfer without directional residue, or outcomes and failures are predicted only by alliance, diagnosis, and treatment dose with no benefit from prior-modality grain.

exact_claim: Spiritual prerequisites are not neutral capacities later applied to insight. Attention, discrimination, trust, longing, self-observation, and integration are trained with a directional grain toward the specific dissolution a tradition seeks, and mismatched grain should predict distinctive resistance and harm patterns.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Vipassana-trained practitioners attempting Advaita self-inquiry should initially keep tracking change and non-ownership rather than resting in witness-recognition. Advaita-trained practitioners attempting vipassana should tend to seek a stable knower or background rather than evenly noting impermanence. Practitioners trained in non-attachment who enter devotional surrender should report affective restraint or suspicion of dependence.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should experience method-specific transfer resistance.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Micro-phenomenological interviews with dual-trained practitioners, coded before doctrinal explanation is invited.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners report smooth switching with no directional pull, or reported resistance is fully explained by temperament, teacher instructions, language, or community identity.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Vivekachudamani-style qualifications should define readiness in terms of the real versus unreal and longing for liberation. Satipatthana and later insight manuals should define readiness through impermanence, suffering, non-ownership, and ethical abandonment. Sufi manuals should orient self-knowledge toward creaturely dependence before God. Dzogchen manuals should orient preparation toward devotion, introduction, recognition, and non-distraction.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Primary manuals should grain prerequisites toward their final dissolution.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind coding of Visuddhimagga chapter 1, Vivekachudamani vv. 17-30, Cloud of Unknowing chapters 3-6, al-Qushayri's Risala, Longchen Rabjam Trekcho, and Satipatthana-related materials.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Prerequisites are generic across manuals, such as calm, attention, ethics, and perseverance, with little relation to the final insight language of each path.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Witness-trained practitioners pushed into rapid de-identification should show witness reification more than dissociation. Non-attachment-trained practitioners attempting devotional surrender should show emotional flattening more than spiritual inflation. Devotional practitioners entering analytic deconstruction should show relational rupture or fear of betrayal more than abstract nihilism.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Adverse effects should cluster by grain mismatch, not only by intensity, trauma history, or lack of prerequisites.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Re-coding VCE-style interview data, retreat adverse-event reports, and teacher case consultations for prior training, current method, failure type, and interpretive frame.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Adverse experiences cluster only by retreat intensity, sleep disruption, psychiatric history, social isolation, or teacher support, with no added explanatory value from prior practice grain.

John Dunne Buddhist Styles of Mindfulness ethics judgment memory classical nondual
