dual trained practitioners Advaita Buddhism vipassana self inquiry practice transfer

near_neighbors.difference: The grain model narrows from experience content to the trained capacity that makes experience or dissolution possible.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The anti-neutrality claim is well known, though the prerequisite-specific formulation has room.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close background structure. Katz argues that mystical experience is shaped by memory, language, expectations, concepts, and tradition-specific frameworks.
near_neighbors.source: Steven T. Katz, Language, Epistemology, and Mysticism, summarized in Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy, Mysticism, https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/mysticism/

near_neighbors.difference: Dunne is intra-Buddhist and practice-style focused. The grain model generalizes across traditions and claims capacities are aimed toward specific dissolutions and failure patterns.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction, especially for the claim that attention and mindfulness are not neutral tools.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close. Dunne distinguishes Classical and Nondual Buddhist styles and shows that mindfulness practices differ in ethics, judgment, present-centered awareness, memory, and theoretical coherence.
near_neighbors.source: John D. Dunne, Buddhist Styles of Mindfulness: A Heuristic Approach, https://www.johnddunne.net/uploads/9/8/5/6/9856107/dunne_j_mindfulness_heuristic.pdf

near_neighbors.difference: Sharf is primarily critical and historical. The grain model is constructive and predictive about prerequisite direction and mismatch harms.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the non-neutral-practice claim, moderate preservation for the predictive grain-mismatch thesis.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Sharf challenges generic meditative experience and generic bare attention, placing practice in historical, ritual, doctrinal, and institutional contexts.
near_neighbors.source: Robert H. Sharf, Buddhist Modernism and the Rhetoric of Meditative Experience, Deep Blue metadata, https://deepblue.lib.umich.edu/items/25b7e9d4-d1f3-4280-8d53-95fa0b53db5c, and Is mindfulness Buddhist?, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25361692/

near_neighbors.difference: Ferrer frames plural religious worlds and participatory events. The grain model focuses on prerequisite structure and the directional shaping of capacities before dissolution.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Participatory theory holds that religious phenomena are cocreated by human cognition, practice, community, and spiritual reality, rather than discovered as a neutral common core.
near_neighbors.source: Jorge Ferrer and colleagues, The Participatory Turn, SUNY Press, https://sunypress.edu/Books/T/The-Participatory-Turn2

near_neighbors.difference: The grain model expands from attentional instruments to all prerequisite capacities and adds a specific adverse-effect prediction.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High internal reduction. Grain is a meaningful development, not an independent discovery.
near_neighbors.overlap: Near exact internal neighbor. It already says contemplative instruments are not separable from doctrine, and that Advaita self-inquiry and vipassana may produce different structural changes masked by coarse reports.
near_neighbors.source: Local Lumenary prior, The Instrument Problem: Attentional Divergence as an Epistemic Variable in Contemplative Convergence, observations/claude/2026-05-25-the-instrument-problem-attentional-divergence-as-an-epistemic-variable-in-contemplative-convergence.md

near_neighbors.difference: VCE does not claim capacities are directionally pre-shaped toward specific dissolutions or that mismatch predicts specific failures such as witness reification versus emotional flatness.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction and a strong test bed.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close empirical-adjacent source. VCE shows that similar meditation-related phenomenology may be interpreted, managed, and valenced differently by tradition, practice, teacher relation, and practitioner factors.
near_neighbors.source: Lindahl et al., The Varieties of Contemplative Experience, PLOS ONE, https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0176239

near_neighbors.difference: Capacity Ledger scores load placement. Grain adds directionality within each load and predicts resistance when a trained direction is repurposed.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high internal reduction. Grain may be the more specific synthesis, but it depends on the same architecture.
near_neighbors.overlap: Internal same-run neighbor. Both ideas use the five capacity variables and compare effort, gift, direct introduction, and practice-realization.
near_neighbors.source: The Capacity Ledger of Gift and Effort, observations/codex/2026-05-26-the-capacity-ledger-of-gift-and-effort.md

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as candidate discovery until dual-trained practitioner reports show directional resistance that cannot be reduced to doctrine, teacher style, or temperament.
Build a manual-coding rubric that separates capacity level from capacity direction, and code whether the direction is explicit in the primary text or inferred from the tradition's final aim.
Treat successful hybrids, smooth cross-training, and Pure Land other-power as anomaly classes, not supporting examples.
Search Hadot on spiritual exercises, Bourdieu on habitus, Sharf, Dunne, Katz, Ferrer, Forman, Taves, and VCE literature before raising novelty.
Run a small practitioner test with at least one teacher each from Theravada, Advaita, Dzogchen or Mahamudra, Sufism, and Pure Land, asking whether grain changes practice sequencing.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.76 to about 0.58. The phrase grain of capacity is generative and the mismatch prediction is useful, but the anti-neutrality structure is strongly anticipated by constructivism, Buddhist mindfulness-style scholarship, participatory theory, adverse meditation research, and local Lumenary attentional-instrument work.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners and scholars say this is just Katz constructivism, Sharf on bare attention, Dunne's mindfulness styles, Ferrer's participatory pluralism, Engler's prerequisites, or ordinary lineage pedagogy. Dual-trained practitioners report no distinctive resistance beyond language and teacher expectations.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can name repeatable, tradition-specific transfer frictions and failure modes that the grain model predicts before doctrinal explanation, and teachers say the rubric improves sequencing and warnings for cross-training students.
questions: When you switch methods, does your trained attention keep moving in the old direction, or is the switch mostly effortless?
Is this idea already obvious as constructivism, practice style, Upaya, adhikara, or teacher pedagogy?
Would naming grain change how you sequence cross-training or diagnose practice failure?

primary_pair: MN 10 Satipatthana Sutta and SN 22.59 Anattalakkhana Sutta, https://suttacentral.net/mn10/en/nyanamoli-thera and https://suttacentral.net/sn22.59/en/bodhi
Vivekachudamani vv. 17-30, https://shlokam.org/text/vivekachudamani.htm
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model may infer final-directional grain from a comparative observer's map rather than from practitioner experience. It also risks using a Madhyamaka fire-and-fuel lens as if all traditions accept dependent co-arising between capacity and dissolution. In Advaita or Sufi frames, sequence, grace, or revelation may be more important than co-arising.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Search found strong near-neighbors in Katz's constructivism, Sharf on meditative experience and bare attention, Dunne on Buddhist styles of mindfulness, Ferrer's participatory theory, Hadot-style spiritual exercises, Engler's developmental prerequisite thesis, Lindahl and Britton's VCE work, and local Lumenary work on attentional instruments and verification architecture. I did not find the exact phrase grain of capacity or a specific claim that prerequisites, rather than experiences, are directionally carved toward the dissolution they enable. Still, most structural components are known.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: Vivekachudamani strongly supports the grain claim because its prerequisites are already defined by the Advaita target: discrimination is framed as conviction that Brahman is real and the world is unreal, and calmness is tied to resting the mind on that goal. MN 10 is less explicit if read alone: it instructs body, feeling, mind, and dhamma contemplation with mindfulness and clear comprehension, but the opening formula does not itself say every act is aimed at non-ownership. SN 22.59 supplies that direction by repeatedly using impermanence, suffering, and non-mastery to reject mine, I, and self. The close reading therefore supports a weaker claim: some traditions explicitly grain prerequisites toward their final insight, while in others the grain must be established by reading practice instructions together with broader doctrinal and soteriological frames.

title: The Grain of Capacity
unlike_statement: Unlike Katz, Sharf, Dunne, Ferrer, Engler, and the local attentional-instrument thesis, this idea says that prerequisites themselves carry a directional grain toward the transformation they enable, and that this grain should predict specific transfer resistance and adverse effects when a practitioner crosses traditions.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Chinul's sudden awakening and gradual cultivation, plus radical Jodo Shinshu Other Power.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence from candidate-discovery range to extended. The rubric remains useful, but only if it becomes stage-sensitive and allows traditions to reject the analyst's ownership vocabulary.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: Chinul makes the same cultivation necessary after awakening but empty of production, so the claimant status changes by stage. Shinran makes even entrusting wholly Other Power, so calling vow, hearing, or trust a support locus may be an analyst's domestication of grace. These cases strain any clean grid that treats support and claimant as stable cells.

claim_units: A path can require real functions while forbidding ordinary self-ownership of those functions.
The right audit unit is a two-part code: support locus plus forbidden claimant.
Right effort, Other Power, direct introduction, and practice-realization differ by which functions are required and which owner is disallowed.
Gift paths should not be translated into hidden self-power, but every path must carry the work somewhere.
Wrong-claimant failures should predict distinctive practice errors and adverse patterns.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Programs with similar behavioral tasks but different claimant rules should produce different failure modes: self-help over-ownership, passive surrender, therapist dependency, medicine-as-savior attribution, or empty compliance with protocol.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Support may be held by client agency, therapist, group, protocol, medicine, higher power, present-session enactment, or integration practice, while some claimant is warned not to take sole credit.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Ordinary skills training shows the same claimant safeguards and failure modes, or therapy outcomes are predicted by alliance, dosage, diagnosis, and social support without any added value from claimant coding.

exact_claim: The effort-gift boundary is not how much the practitioner does, but who is allowed to claim the doing. Each path requires functions such as receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration, while assigning their support to a practitioner, teacher, vow, name, text, community, present act, awareness itself, or later integration, and explicitly forbidding some claimant from owning the function.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Pure Land texts warn against self-power calculation, Zen texts warn against gaining idea or contrivance, Advaita texts warn against egoic doership or premature realization, and effort-centered Buddhist texts warn against claiming aggregates as self while still authorizing cultivation.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Practice manuals should warn not only about missing capacity, but about a necessary function being claimed by the wrong owner.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind coding of manuals from Jodo Shinshu, Theravada, Soto Zen, Dzogchen, Advaita, and Sri Vaishnava prapatti.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Manual warnings cluster around generic ethics, obedience, humility, or community order, with no recurring relation to wrong-claimant patterns.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Practitioners distinguish felt modes such as I cultivate, I entrust, I recognize, I just sit, or this is not mine, and they can name which phrasing would feel doctrinally or practically wrong in each method.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should report shifts in permitted first-person grammar when changing methods.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Micro-phenomenological interviews with dual-trained practitioners, coded before doctrinal explanation is invited.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners report only generic differences in calm, concentration, devotion, or teacher style, with no felt shift in agency or claimant grammar.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Gift practice shows self-power anxiety or false surrender, direct-introduction practice shows reification of recognition, practice-realization shows achievement seeking or empty formalism, and effort practice shows pride or mechanical striving.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Adverse practice events should sometimes cluster around wrong-claimant failures.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Re-coding VCE-style interviews and teacher case consultations for prior training, current method, claimant language, and failure type.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Adverse reports are fully explained by trauma history, retreat intensity, sleep loss, psychiatric vulnerability, poor supervision, or social isolation, with no added value from claimant coding.

near_neighbors.difference: The claimant rubric moves from coping and perceived control to soteriological practice functions, and adds the negative field of which owner is forbidden to claim a function.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The main variable, allocation of responsibility, is already modeled empirically.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close structural neighbor. Pargament's self-directing, deferring, and collaborative styles already ask whether responsibility for change rests with the person, God, or both.
near_neighbors.source: Kenneth Pargament et al., religious coping styles, summarized in https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC1630686/ and https://link.springer.com/rwe/10.1007/978-3-030-24348-7_578

near_neighbors.difference: Locus-of-control literature does not usually close-read practice manuals or distinguish support holder from forbidden claimant in contemplative training.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for support-locus originality, moderate preservation for the manual-coding rubric.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Internal, external, God-empowered, and God-deferred control map directly onto support locus and agency attribution.
near_neighbors.source: Religious locus of control and spiritual-health locus of control, overview at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Locus_of_control and related God-control literature

near_neighbors.difference: The Lumenary idea generalizes this to multiple functions and traditions rather than keeping it inside Jodo Shinshu.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The forbidden-claimant idea is strongly present in Shinran.
near_neighbors.overlap: Near exact for source-centered paths. Shinran already separates entrusting from self-powered calculation and treats the practicer's claim as the problem.
near_neighbors.source: Shinran, Other Power and hakarai, https://shinranworks.com/glossary/ and https://shinranworks.com/letters/a-collection-of-letters-zensho-text/what-we-call-other-power/

near_neighbors.difference: The claimant rubric is broader than Christian regeneration and allows non-theistic and activity-centered support loci.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the core agency question, some novelty remains in cross-tradition operationalization.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. The debate asks whether salvation is God's work alone or cooperative, and whether faith itself is caused by divine action.
near_neighbors.source: Christian monergism and synergism, overview at https://www.monergism.com/what-monergism-vs-synergism

near_neighbors.difference: The rubric adds multiple support functions and forbidden claimant warnings beyond a two-model grace debate.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction, especially for the claim that traditions themselves divide over this variable.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close indigenous near-neighbor. The monkey and kitten analogies already encode different distributions of effort and grace.
near_neighbors.source: Sri Vaishnava markata and marjara nyaya, example overview at https://philosophy.institute/indian-philosophy/visistadvaita-path-liberation-bhakti-prapatti/

near_neighbors.difference: Upaya does not by itself ask who may claim the required function, only which means fits the practitioner.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close background. Upaya already treats teaching and practice as adapted to capacities, safeguards, and current limitations.
near_neighbors.source: Buddhist upaya and capacity-sensitive teaching, Bhikkhu Analayo, Skill in Means and Mindfulness, https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s12671-021-01795-2, and Harvard Divinity Bulletin on Buddhist skillful means, https://bulletin.hds.harvard.edu/buddhist-studies-the-buddhist-way/

near_neighbors.difference: The new rubric adds a sharper prohibited-owner column, but mostly develops the same architecture.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Very high internal reduction. This should be merged rather than published as independent.
near_neighbors.overlap: Near exact internal neighbor. The capacity ledger already asks where receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration are held across practitioner, vow, teacher, present act, and post-insight integration.
near_neighbors.source: Local Lumenary prior, The Capacity Ledger of Gift and Effort, plus The Grain of Capacity, cited in the prompt

near_neighbors.difference: Chinul gives a stage-sensitive soteriology, not a cross-tradition function grid.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction and strong pressure to make the rubric stage-based.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close anomaly-neighbor. Sudden awakening followed by gradual cultivation already requires necessary practice without treating practice as ultimate production.
near_neighbors.source: Chinul, Secrets on Cultivating the Mind, https://www.themathesontrust.org/papers/buddhism/Chinul-Secrets_on_Cultivating_the_Mind.pdf

next_loop_instructions: Merge this record with the capacity-ledger and credit-distribution records rather than publishing it as independent.
Read Pargament et al. on religious problem-solving styles directly before claiming novelty.
Build the coding sheet with fields for function, stage, support locus, forbidden claimant, safeguard, and failure mode.
Code primary manuals before broad tradition labels, especially SN 45.8, Shinran letters, Tannisho, Bendowa, Chinul, Longchen Rabjam, and Sri Vaishnava prapatti sources.
Add an explicit value for traditions or stages that reject the claimant frame itself.
Do not promote above extended unless practitioner interviews show the rubric predicts sequencing or failure patterns not already explained by religious coping, upaya, or lineage pedagogy.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.57 to about 0.45. The prohibited-claimant column is useful, but the main structure is strongly anticipated by self-power and other-power, monergism and synergism, Pargament's religious coping styles, locus-of-control research, Sri Vaishnava grace-effort debates, and local capacity-ledger work.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Teachers say the rubric merely renames standard warnings, or psychologists say it is Pargament and locus-of-control language applied to contemplative texts. Practitioners find claimant language alien or misleading.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Teachers can use the grid to predict previously missed failure modes, especially in cross-training, and can identify function-specific wrong-claimant warnings in primary manuals before seeing the model.
questions: Is support locus plus forbidden claimant already obvious as self-power, other-power, doership, gaining idea, pride, upaya, or ordinary lineage pedagogy?
For receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration, can you name who or what carries the function in your training, and who must not claim it?
Would this rubric change how you sequence practice, diagnose student errors, or warn cross-trained practitioners?

primary_pair: SN 45.8 Magga-vibhanga Sutta, right effort, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/SN/SN45_8.html
Shinran, Letter to Senshin, Other Power, https://shinranworks.com/letters/a-collection-of-letters-zensho-text/what-we-call-other-power/
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. Claimant language may impose an ownership grammar on traditions that speak in terms of karma, grace, vow, emptiness, devotion, non-contrivance, or conventional truth. The model also risks flattening stage changes: the same action may be self-power before awakening, natural expression after awakening, and conventional compassion in public teaching.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The closest structural gap is not self-power versus other-power, which is well known, but the per-function grid of support locus plus prohibited claimant. Still, Pargament's religious coping styles, religious locus of control, Christian monergism and synergism, Sri Vaishnava markata and marjara, Buddhist upaya, Engler's ego-strength thesis, and prior Lumenary capacity-ledger work already occupy much of the architecture.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 45.8 makes the practitioner the grammatical agent of desire, persistence, intent, prevention, abandonment, cultivation, and maintenance. Shinran's Letter to Senshin makes settled shinjin incompatible with calculation on the practicer's side, and says no working is true working. This comparison supports the function-claimant split: practice can retain necessary operations while changing who may claim them. But the split is already explicit in the primary materials, especially in Shinran, so the rubric mostly generalizes an old tension rather than discovering it.

title: The Forbidden Claimant Rubric
unlike_statement: Unlike Pargament, religious locus-of-control work, self-power and other-power debates, monergism and synergism, and the local capacity ledger, this idea says that each required practice function should be coded twice: where the function is operationally supported and which claimant the tradition forbids from owning it.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: The Bhagavad Gita itself is the strongest anomaly to the clean typology.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence in tradition-level categories. The model should become a stage, verse, and commentary-level coding scheme before public use.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: The model uses BG 3.27 to classify the Gita as disowning doership, but BG 18.14 gives a five-cause account that includes doer, effort, instruments, body, and divine factor. BG 2.47 also commands action while refusing claim on fruits. This means the same text can be practitioner-active, anti-ego, split-credit, and divine-inclusive depending on verse and commentary.

claim_units: The question whose power drives transformation is a practice instruction, not only a doctrine.
Self-power versus other-power is too narrow, because traditions distribute credit across multiple agents and stages.
Five patterns can be distinguished: practitioner-centered, source-centered, split-credit, activity-centered, and ground-centered.
SN 45.8 and Bhagavad Gita 3.27 show similar outward diligence with different agency orientation.
Each credit type should predict a characteristic failure mode and matching safeguard.
Credit distribution is separable from the capacity ledger, because two paths can require similar functions but credit their source differently.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Organizational change and clinical recovery programs
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Programs with similar tasks but different credit stories should show different failure patterns: burnout from self-credit, passivity from source-credit, performative compliance from activity-credit, and premature completion claims from ground-credit.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Change programs distribute credit among individual effort, facilitator, group, protocol, medication, higher power, emergent process, or already-present capacity.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Outcomes and failures are explained by resources, relationship quality, severity, dosage, incentives, and social support, with no independent effect from the credit story.

exact_claim: Credit distribution is a separable practice variable. Traditions distribute the power behind transformation across practitioner-centered, source-centered, split-credit, activity-centered, and ground-centered patterns, and those patterns shape the felt quality of effort, failure meanings, safeguards, and practice dangers.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Practitioner-centered texts emphasize warnings against pride and attainment ownership; source-centered texts emphasize warnings against false surrender, passivity, or antinomianism; split-credit texts warn against outcome attachment; activity-centered texts warn against gaining idea and formalism; ground-centered texts warn against premature realization claims.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Manuals grouped by credit type should show matching safeguard profiles.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind coding of at least three manuals or teaching corpora per proposed type.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Each tradition warns equally against all failure modes, or safeguard profiles track institution, teacher temperament, ethics, or historical polemic better than credit type.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: A practitioner trained in Theravada right effort and Jodo Shinshu entrusting, or Advaita inquiry and Soto just-sitting, can describe different felt meanings of effort, failure, and credit without being prompted with the typology.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should experience different agency orientations when switching practices.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Micro-phenomenological interviews with dual-trained practitioners and teachers.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners report no felt agency shift, or the shift is fully explained by vocabulary, teacher style, devotional temperament, or institutional identity.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Two traditions can require similar receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration functions while crediting the power behind those functions differently, and this difference predicts different safeguards.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Credit distribution should be separable from the capacity ledger.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Paired coding of Jodo Shinshu and Christian grace traditions, Soto Zen and Dzogchen, Theravada and Gita karma-yoga, with independent raters.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Credit distribution always collapses into support locus, or support-locus coding predicts safeguards and failures without a separate credit variable.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Manuals and teachers mark special danger when a practitioner moves from effortful preparation to recognition, surrender, no-gaining practice, or already-accomplished realization.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Stage transitions should be high-risk points when credit distribution changes.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Stage-coded readings of Theravada, Sufi, Advaita, Dzogchen, Soto, and Pure Land training sequences.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Transitions do not show distinctive warnings, or reported difficulties are better predicted by retreat intensity, trauma, doctrine learning, or social pressure.

primary_pair: SN 45.8 Magga-vibhanga Sutta, https://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/sn/sn45/sn45.008.than.html
Bhagavad Gita 3.27 and 18.14, https://www.bhagavad-gita.us/bhagavad-gita-3-27/ and https://www.bhagavad-gita.us/bhagavad-gita-18-14/
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The five types may classify traditions at too coarse a level. Many traditions shift credit by stage, genre, audience, and commentator. The Gita is internally multi-causal; Sufism distinguishes stations and states; Advaita distinguishes preparation from recognition; Buddhism authorizes effort conventionally while undermining self-ownership analytically.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The strongest prior art is Pargament's self-directing, deferring, and collaborative religious coping; Christian monergism and synergism; Japanese Buddhist jiriki and tariki; Sri Vaishnava markata and marjara; Sufi maqam and hal; and religious-attribution or God-control psychology. The searched literature did not show the exact five-type typology with activity-centered and ground-centered categories plus failure-mode predictions, but the main agency-allocation variable is well established.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: The SN 45.8 comparison is strong but slightly overstated. SN 45.8 makes effort operationally direct: the practitioner generates desire, endeavors, activates persistence, upholds, and exerts intent. Bhagavad Gita 3.27 denies egoic sole-doership by assigning actions to prakriti's gunas, but Bhagavad Gita 18.14 complicates this by listing five causes of action, including body, doer, senses, efforts, and divine factor. The better close-read is not that the Gita simply disowns effort, but that it embeds effort in a multi-causal anti-ego framework. This supports credit distribution, but weakens the clean split-credit classification.

title: Whose Doing Is This? Credit Distribution as a Practice Variable
unlike_statement: Unlike Pargament, monergism and synergism, self-power and other-power, and markata and marjara debates, this idea adds activity-centered and ground-centered patterns, treats credit attribution as a felt practice variable, and predicts matching failure modes and safeguards across multiple traditions.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Radical Jodo Shinshu, especially Tannisho chapters 8 and 10, where nembutsu is not the practitioner's practice or good act and no working is true working.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence in universality and requires a refusal-category. The idea remains useful as a coding heuristic, but not as a claim that all traditions perform a handoff.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If even hearing, saying, entrusting, and gratitude are Amida's working rather than trained human functions, then the analyst's transfer language may smuggle self-power into a tradition built to reject that framing. Dogen also strains the model because practice-realization collapses bridge and arrival rather than transferring ownership from one holder to another.

claim_units: Effort and gift should be modeled as timed transfer rather than fixed opposition.
Traditions train functions such as receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration, then may forbid ordinary-self ownership of those same functions.
Other-power, direct-introduction, effort-centered, and practice-realization paths differ by transfer point, support holder, credited source, forbidden claimant, safeguard, and repair.
This can become a coding rubric for manuals and practitioner interviews.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Methods that move agency from therapist or group to client, or from willpower to acceptance, should show special safeguards at transfer points, such as relapse planning, integration, alliance repair, and warnings against premature independence.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Identity-changing work trains capacities that later must be relocated from client ego, therapist, group, medication, protocol, insight, or ongoing practice.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Ordinary skill acquisition shows the same warnings, or outcomes are fully explained by severity, alliance, dosage, social support, and resources without any transfer-specific signal.

exact_claim: The useful comparative variable is not effort versus gift on a fixed scale, but the stage at which a trained function becomes unclaimable by the practitioner, who or what then carries it, and which safeguards appear when that transfer fails.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Pure Land texts should warn against calculation, Dzogchen texts against contrivance and premature recognition, Soto texts against gaining idea, early Buddhist texts against appropriation of aggregates or attainment ownership, and Gita-style karma-yoga texts against fruit ownership.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Manuals should mark transfer points with special safeguards rather than generic warnings.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind stage-coded manual analysis across at least three texts per tradition.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Warnings cluster by institution, teacher temperament, ethics, or historical polemic rather than by transfer point.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Interviewees can distinguish I cultivate, I entrust, recognition happens, and just sitting enacts, without being prompted with the rubric.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should report shifts in permitted first-person grammar when changing methods.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Micro-phenomenological interviews with dual-trained practitioners and teachers.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners report no felt shift, or shifts are fully explained by vocabulary, teacher style, temperament, or institutional identity.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Two practices with the same broad credit type but different transfer timing show different safeguards and repair instructions.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The handoff rubric should add predictive value beyond Pargament-style responsibility allocation and the local capacity ledger.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Inter-rater coding of matched pairs such as Soto and Dzogchen, Pure Land and Christian grace, Theravada and karma-yoga.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Support locus and credit-distribution coding predict the same outcomes without needing handoff timing.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Self-power calculation is repaired by hearing or entrusting, contrivance by relaxing into introduced awareness, gaining idea by just-sitting correction, and attainment ownership by no-self or humility teachings.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Failed transfers should produce type-specific repair practices.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Teacher interviews and case reports from retreat, pastoral, and sangha settings.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Repair practices do not map to transfer failures, or all traditions prescribe the same repairs for the same student problems.

primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model risks imposing a staged transfer on traditions that deny transfer, collapse before and after, or shift grammar by genre and audience. Longchenpa's Trekcho gives recognition without contrived effort but also gives preliminaries, devotion, vows, conduct, and post-recognition practice, which means production is denied only at some moments, not globally.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The search found close prior structures in religious coping styles, self-power and other-power, Dogen practice-realization, Dzogchen direct introduction, Engler on ego strength before no-self, the Buddhist raft and upaya pattern, and local Lumenary capacity-ledger and credit-distribution work. I did not find the exact field list as a cross-tradition coding sheet, but the core agency-allocation and means-end reversal are well established.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 45.8 makes right effort operational and practitioner-facing: desire, endeavor, persistence, intent, and maintenance of skillful qualities are all assigned to the monk's practice field. Tannisho reverses the claimant grammar: nembutsu is not a practice or good act when it arises from Other Power, and claims such as my disciples are rejected because the working belongs to Amida. The comparison supports a real difference in claimant policy, but Tannisho is not a late handoff after training. It often refuses self-claim from the entrance, so the model needs a no-transfer or analyst-frame-refused value.

title: The Handoff Point Is The Variable
unlike_statement: Unlike Pargament, self-power and other-power debates, Dogen practice-realization, and local Lumenary credit models, this idea says the decisive unit is a timed transfer for each required function, with observable safeguards and repairs at that transfer point.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Mencian Confucian cultivation and inherited ritual traditions, where the path starts from innate moral sprouts, family formation, ritual participation, or ordinary socialization rather than a dramatic break in incapacity.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence in the universality claim but strengthens the model if revised as a variable with none, mild, gradual, social, gift, grace, and self-disclosure values.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If some traditions assume an already-open moral capacity, then first-break is not universal. Also, SN 55.5 strains the model because social encounter comes first, but appropriate attention and practice are immediately active. This leaves a residual question: what enables recognition of the person of integrity? The model either needs latent capacity, no-break, or gradual-break values, or it generates infinite regress.

claim_units: There is a structural circularity at the beginning of transformative spiritual practice.
Traditions can be compared by their first-break mechanism: social encounter, cosmic gift, prevenient grace, self-disclosure, and possibly no-break or gradual-break cases.
The severity of the circularity tracks anthropology, especially whether human nature is seen as capable, impaired, bound, sinful, or already endowed.
The entry solution predicts effort theory, verification, characteristic failure, and safeguards later in the path.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Programs aimed at distorted thinking, addiction, trauma, or critical thinking should have identifiable first-break mechanisms and entry safeguards. For example, readiness work, alliance formation, motivational interviewing, peer sponsor encounter, or disorienting dilemma should predict downstream practice architecture.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: A process that changes the very capacities needed to enter it must explain how participation begins: through trusted encounter, crisis, institutional scaffolding, latent capacity, surrender, medication, group containment, or gradual readiness building.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Entry success and downstream structure are fully explained by alliance, severity, resources, coercion, incentives, dosage, and social support, with no added predictive power from first-break type.

exact_claim: Spiritual paths face an upstream circularity: the very person needing transformation lacks the wisdom, freedom, faith, or recognition needed to begin. Traditions solve this first-break problem through mechanisms such as social encounter, cosmic gift, prevenient grace, or self-disclosure, and the chosen solution predicts downstream practice architecture.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Jodo Shinshu warns against self-power calculation, Orange-Augustinian grace traditions warn against pride and presumption, Dzogchen warns against premature recognition and fabrication, and social-encounter models warn against isolation from good friends or teachers.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Traditions with severe first-break solutions should show entry-point safeguards that match the feared false beginning.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Stage-coded analysis of entry instructions, initiation manuals, sermons, and teacher warnings.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Entry warnings are no stronger or more type-specific than in mild or no-break traditions, or they track institution and polemic better than first-break mechanism.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Traditions with deep incapacity, sin, mappo, avidya as pervasive delusion, or ordinary mind as unreliable should use more external, prior, or self-disclosing breaks than traditions with innate goodness or workable capacities.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Anthropology should predict first-break severity.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Comparative coding of primary texts and doctrinal manuals across Confucian, Theravada, Pure Land, Christian, Dzogchen, Yogacara, and Zen sources.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Optimistic anthropologies use severe external first-break mechanisms just as often, or pessimistic anthropologies use ordinary self-cultivation without special entry safeguards.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Movements that combine social encounter with gradual cultivation, or self-disclosure with original-endowment views, should stabilize more readily than hybrids that combine radical other-power with strongly self-powered entry practice.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Historical hybrids should be more stable when their first-break mechanisms are compatible.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Historical comparison of Chan, Pure Land-Zen syntheses, Christian contemplative revivals, and modern nondual movements.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Hybrid success is better predicted by patronage, institutions, language, politics, charisma, or ritual compatibility than by first-break type.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: They can describe whether the first move is meeting, hearing, being called, being enabled, surrendering, recognizing what was present, cultivating a seed, or simply continuing inherited practice.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Practitioners should recognize entry grammar differences even when they do not use philosophical circularity language.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Practitioner and teacher interviews, coded without revealing the typology.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners across traditions describe entry in generic terms, and teacher guidance does not change according to first-break grammar.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate applies the structure to soteriological transformation, will, faith, and practice architecture rather than knowledge of virtue alone.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the core circularity, moderate remaining novelty for the cross-tradition practice mapping.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for the formal bootstrapping problem: how one can inquire into what one does not know, and how one will recognize it when found.
near_neighbors.source: Plato, Meno, 80d-e and recollection argument, https://classics.mit.edu/Plato/meno.html

near_neighbors.difference: Augustine is a single-tradition theological solution, not a comparative typology across Buddhist, Pure Land, Christian, and Dzogchen cases.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Very high reduction for the prevenient-grace category.
near_neighbors.overlap: Near exact for the Christian version: the beginning of faith is God's gift, and even thinking with assent requires divine sufficiency.

near_neighbors.overlap: Near exact for first-break logic. The canons deny that prayer, desire, striving, seeking, assent, or the beginning of faith arise by unaided nature.

near_neighbors.difference: James offers a psychology of conversion, not a typology of first-break mechanisms across practice manuals with anthropological severity predictions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for active versus surrender entry patterns.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. James already distinguishes volitional and self-surrender conversion and treats conversion as the unification of a divided self, gradual or sudden.

near_neighbors.difference: Rambo's model is social-psychological and process-oriented. It does not organize traditions by metaphysical first-break solution or use anthropology severity as the main variable.

near_neighbors.overlap: Close for conversion architecture: context, crisis, quest, encounter, interaction, commitment, and consequences already map entry conditions and downstream change.

near_neighbors.difference: Rahner gives one Christian ontological solution, while the candidate compares multiple traditions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the acceptance-made-possible structure.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for the claim that God's self-communication is also the condition that makes acceptance possible.
near_neighbors.source: Karl Rahner, supernatural existential summary, https://people.bu.edu/wwildman/bce/rahner.htm

near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for the paradox of will, receptivity, negative effort, and grace in spiritual exercise.
near_neighbors.source: Simone Kotva, Effort and Grace, reviewed at https://attentionsw.org/the-paradox-of-will-and-grace-a-review-of-kotvas-effort-and-grace/

near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction and a typology correction.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for transformation of the basis, where the cognitive basis that generates delusion must be overturned for awakening.
near_neighbors.source: Yogacara asraya-paravrtti, overview at https://encyclopediaofbuddhism.org/wiki/%C4%80%C5%9Brayapar%C4%81v%E1%B9%9Btti

near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction to universality. It forces a no-break or already-open category.

next_loop_instructions: Revise the typology to include no-break, gradual-break, inherited-practice, social-encounter, cosmic-gift, prevenient-grace, self-disclosure, and undecidable values.
Separate the formal circularity from practitioner-lived salience. A tradition may structurally imply a first-break without practitioners treating it as a problem.
Close-read Tannisho chapter 1, Orange canons 3-7, SN 55.5, Mencius 2A6, Longchenpa Trekcho, and a Yogacara transformation-of-basis source before publication.
Read Rambo and William James directly before claiming novelty in conversion architecture.
Test whether anthropology predicts first-break type without circular scoring. Define anthropology independently before coding break mechanism.
Use practitioner interviews to ask for entry failure cases, not abstract agreement with the paradox.
Do not promote above novel_synthesis unless the model predicts safeguards or hybrid stability better than conversion studies, grace theology, and transformative learning theory.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.78 to about 0.62. The cross-tradition variable and downstream prediction are promising, but the first-break circularity is strongly anticipated by Meno's paradox, Augustine and Orange on the beginning of faith, James and Rambo on conversion, Rahner, Kotva, Yogacara, Dzogchen pointing-out, and transformative learning. Do not call it a discovery until the typology outperforms these prior frames in manual coding and practitioner tests.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners and scholars say this is already Meno, initium fidei, conversion studies, self-power and other-power, original enlightenment, or transformative learning. Practitioners do not recognize the circularity as guiding actual pedagogy.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can identify type-specific entry risks and say the rubric clarifies why methods that look similar later in the path diverge sharply at entry, especially in cross-training and hybrid settings.
questions: Is the first-break problem a live practical issue in your tradition, or mainly an outsider's philosophical reconstruction?
What first move is possible for someone still trapped in the condition the path diagnoses?
Does your tradition's answer to that question predict later warnings, verification methods, and failure modes?
Would this typology change how you compare traditions, or does it rename known categories such as grace, conversion, encounter, original enlightenment, and moral sprouts?

Augustinian, Catholic, Orthodox, Wesleyan, and Reformed theologians of grace

primary_pair: SN 55.5, To Sariputta, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/SN/SN55_5.html
Second Council of Orange, canons 3-7, https://www.crivoice.org/creedorange.html
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: Moderate to high. The model may project a philosophical bootstrapping problem onto practitioners who begin through family, ritual, obedience, attraction, crisis, community, or habit without experiencing a paradox. It may also collapse distinct cases: Shinran's Other Power and Orange's prevenient grace are structurally close, while Dzogchen self-disclosure and Platonic recollection may be closer than the typology admits.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The strongest prior art includes Plato's Meno on inquiry into what one does not know, Augustine and Orange on initium fidei, William James on volitional and self-surrender conversion, Rambo's conversion stages, Rahner's supernatural existential, Simone Kotva on effort and grace, Yogacara asraya-paravrtti, Dzogchen pointing-out literature, and transformative learning theory. The audit did not find the exact four-value cross-tradition typology with downstream predictions, but most structural ingredients are already named in adjacent literatures.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 55.5 lists association with people of integrity first, followed by listening, appropriate attention, and practice in accordance with the Dhamma. This supports an external entry condition, but it does not explicitly say the practitioner is incapable of appropriate attention without grace. Orange is much stronger: even prayer, will, desire, striving, seeking, and the beginning of faith are gifts of grace. The comparison supports a spectrum of first-break severity, but weakens the claim that the same paradox is equally live in both texts. SN 55.5 may be a conditional path model, while Orange is an explicit anti-self-initiation doctrine.

title: The First-Break Problem
unlike_statement: Unlike Meno, Augustine, Orange, James, Rambo, Rahner, Kotva, Yogacara, and Dzogchen sources, this idea claims that the first-break mechanism is a cross-tradition variable that predicts later effort theory, verification, safeguards, and failure modes. That comparative-predictive use is the main contribution, not the circularity itself.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Dogen's practice-realization and other no-boundary traditions where decisive transformation is not approached through a separable practitioner-event interface.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: This lowers originality and model confidence. The idea remains useful as a careful comparative heuristic, but it should be classed as extended unless it replaces interface with coding values such as no-boundary, collapsed-boundary, practice-realization, vow-mediated, teacher-mediated, and analyst-frame-refused.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If practice and realization are one, then an interface between practitioner and transformation may be an imposed dualism. A self-limitation marker may not be enough because the problematic category is not only the map, it is the boundary implied by interface.

claim_units: Comparison must first preserve the transformation register, such as human effort, Other Power, primordial awareness, practice-realization, divine restoration, or causal continuity.
Comparison should then code only tradition-recognized vulnerabilities and safeguards around the transformation event or condition.
Relevant vulnerabilities include hearing, doubt, calculation, misrecognition, false trust, teacher relation, ethical misuse, memory, discernment, community correction, and integration.
The rubric needs a self-limitation marker for traditions that say analysis, conceptualization, or mapping can become part of the obstacle.
The model rejects a universal human-capacity ledger and instead asks whose act transformation is said to be, what can obscure or counterfeit it, and where analysis must yield to practice.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Different change registers should produce different warning profiles: CBT should warn about distorted appraisal and homework nonuse, psychoanalysis about resistance and enactment, somatic therapy about dysregulation and bypassing body signals, twelve-step work about self-will and isolation, ACT about experiential avoidance and fusion.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: First identify the method's claimed change register, such as client skill acquisition, therapeutic relationship, unconscious integration, nervous-system regulation, medication effect, group accountability, surrender, or acceptance. Then map only the vulnerabilities and safeguards that method itself recognizes, including warnings that analysis can block the process.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Failure warnings do not cluster by change register, or ordinary modality labels, therapist culture, client severity, or institutional setting explain warnings better than the register-sensitive model.

exact_claim: A path should be compared in two ordered layers: first identify the tradition's own register for decisive transformation, then map only the vulnerabilities and safeguards the tradition itself names at the human boundary of that transformation, including a marker for cases where the mapping stance itself becomes an obstacle.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Tannisho, Shinran letters, and Jodo Shinshu teaching materials should warn more against self-power, calculation, pride in practice, and self-assessment than against laxity as the primary danger.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Other Power texts should show a distinctive warning profile after the register is identified.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind coding of Jodo Shinshu primary texts and manuals against Theravada, Yogic, and monastic effort manuals.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Other Power materials do not warn against calculation more than effort-centered manuals, or the warnings track genre, polemic, or institution better than transformation register.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Dzogchen and Mahamudra materials should warn especially against misrecognition, mistaking an experience for recognition, reifying awareness, inadequate teacher relation, and failure to integrate conduct.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Direct-introduction traditions should show a warning profile distinct from Other Power traditions.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Longchenpa, pointing-out manuals, Mahamudra manuals, Tergar or other teacher-facing materials, and practitioner interviews.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Their warnings are generic morality or effort warnings, or the same profile appears regardless of claimed transformation register.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Dogen and Soto materials should correct before-after, means-end, and practitioner-event separations more often than texts framed by gradual effort, vow, grace, or recognition.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Practice-realization traditions should resist interface grammar more than causal-cultivation or divine-gift traditions.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Bendowa, Genjokoan, selected Shobogenzo fascicles, Soto practice manuals, and teacher interviews.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practice-realization texts freely use interface-like causal language without corrective pressure, or their corrections are no stronger than in unrelated traditions.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Independent coders should infer later manual warnings from early register statements at above-chance rates, for example calculation in Shinran, misrecognition in Dzogchen, wrong effort in gradual paths, and conceptual reification in apophatic or raft-like traditions.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: A register-sensitive rubric should predict warning profiles better than direct summary or agency-locus models.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Inter-rater manual-coding trial across at least six traditions, with prediction scoring before coders read later warning sections.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Predictions are vague, post hoc, low agreement, or no better than Pargament-style agency categories, direct textual summary, or broad tradition labels.

Tannisho chapter 8 nembutsu not practice good act Shinran pdf
Dogen Bendowa practice-realization training and enlightenment are one text
Oliver Freiberger Elements of a Comparative Methodology in the Study of Religion
Francis Clooney Comparative Theology Deep Learning Across Religious Borders method
Paul Hedges Comparative Theology and Hermeneutics Gadamer interreligious interpretation
Steven Katz Language Epistemology and Mysticism no pure unmediated experience
Robert Sharf Buddhist Modernism and the Rhetoric of Meditative Experience pdf
Pargament religion problem-solving process three styles of coping
Lindbeck Nature of Doctrine cultural linguistic model doctrine rules

near_neighbors.difference: Freiberger gives a general comparative methodology, not a transformation-register rubric focused on practitioner vulnerabilities, safeguards, and self-limitation warnings.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The candidate's ordered method is not new in form. Remaining novelty depends on whether the register and warning-profile fields predict outcomes that Freiberger's general frame does not specify.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close at the method level. Freiberger already frames comparison as a disciplined sequence involving configuration, selection, description, juxtaposition, redescription, rectification, and theory formation.
near_neighbors.source: Oliver Freiberger, Elements of a Comparative Methodology in the Study of Religion, https://www.mdpi.com/2077-1444/9/2/38

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate is more operational: it asks coders to name transformation register, interface vulnerabilities, safeguards, and analyst-frame refusal.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction. The idea may be a practical coding extension of comparative theology rather than a new discovery.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Comparative theology already requires patient attention to particular texts, hermeneutic translation, tradition-specific meaning, and vulnerability to being corrected by the other tradition.
near_neighbors.source: Paul Hedges, Comparative Theology and Hermeneutics, https://www.mdpi.com/2077-1444/7/1/7, with Francis X. Clooney's Comparative Theology, https://francisclooney.hsites.harvard.edu/publications/comparative-theology-deep-learning-across-religious-borders

near_neighbors.difference: Katz argues for contextual mediation of mystical experience. The candidate turns contextual caution into a practice-manual rubric and adds predictions about warnings and safeguards.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for register sensitivity, moderate remaining novelty for predictive coding.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for the warning against comparing experiences or transformations apart from the concepts and tradition that shape them.
near_neighbors.source: Steven T. Katz, Language, Epistemology, and Mysticism, discussed at https://www.mdpi.com/2077-1444/13/10/983 and https://people.bu.edu/wwildman/relexp/reviews/review_katz01.htm

near_neighbors.difference: Sharf is mainly critical and historical, while the candidate proposes a constructive rubric for cross-tradition comparison.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction. The model must show that it avoids the reification Sharf criticizes.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Sharf warns against reducing Buddhist path terms to generic inner experiences and against modern extraction of meditative states from doctrinal, ritual, and institutional settings.
near_neighbors.source: Robert H. Sharf, Buddhist Modernism and the Rhetoric of Meditative Experience, https://deepblue.lib.umich.edu/items/25b7e9d4-d1f3-4280-8d53-95fa0b53db5c

near_neighbors.difference: Lindbeck is a theology of doctrine and ecumenical interpretation, not a coding method for transformation registers and failure warnings across practice manuals.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The candidate extends a known grammar-sensitive stance into a narrower practitioner-facing tool.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Lindbeck's cultural-linguistic approach treats doctrines as rules within religious communities, which anticipates the idea that transformation claims must be read inside their own grammar.
near_neighbors.source: George Lindbeck, The Nature of Doctrine, cultural-linguistic model, https://openlibrary.org/works/OL5607588W/The_nature_of_doctrine

near_neighbors.difference: Pargament is psychological and coping-focused. The candidate uses primary-text registers of salvation, awakening, or realization and asks what vulnerabilities each tradition itself names.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The model must prove it predicts more than agency locus or coping style.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for agency distribution. Pargament already distinguishes self-directing, deferring, and collaborative religious problem-solving styles involving different relationships between person and God.
near_neighbors.source: Pargament et al., Religion and the Problem-Solving Process: Three Styles of Coping, https://icmglt.org/icmlibrary/religion-and-the-problem-solving-process-three-styles-of-coping/

near_neighbors.difference: MN 22 is a Buddhist internal teaching about the right use of doctrine, not a comparative rubric field.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the self-limitation clause. Moderate remaining novelty for making self-limitation a required comparative field.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for self-limitation. The text explicitly treats right teaching as something that must be grasped properly and then released, while warning against wrong grasping of the Dhamma.
near_neighbors.source: MN 22, The Water-Snake Simile, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/MN/MN22.html

near_neighbors.difference: Dzogchen sources provide one register and its risks. The candidate compares that register with Other Power, practice-realization, divine restoration, and effortful paths without reducing them to one agency model.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. It supports usefulness but not uniqueness.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for primordial-awareness interface risks: recognition, misrecognition, teacher mediation, expectation of special experience, and the need to stabilize recognition.
near_neighbors.source: Dzogchen pointing-out practitioner sources, example Tergar on pointing out true nature, https://learning.tergar.org/course_library/mingyur-rinpoches-monthly-teachings/mt-august-2019-pointing-out-your-true-nature/

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as a metaphysical claim. Treat it as a comparative and practitioner-facing coding heuristic.

Replace the single term interface with coded values such as boundary, no-boundary, collapsed-boundary, teacher-mediated, vow-mediated, practice-realization, self-disclosure, and analyst-frame-refused.
Build a rubric with fields for decisive register, named vulnerabilities, named safeguards, self-limitation warning, analyst-frame refusal, and observable failure modes.

Test primary texts before broad tradition labels: Tannisho chapters 1, 6, 8, 10, and 11; Shinran letters; Bendowa or Genjokoan; one Dzogchen or Mahamudra pointing-out manual; one Christian grace or apophatic source; and one Sufi fana or baqa source.
Run a blind prediction test: code register from early doctrinal statements, predict later warning profiles, then compare against actual manual warnings.
Ask practitioners for concrete student failure cases and repair instructions rather than abstract agreement with the model.

Read Freiberger, Clooney, Hedges, Katz, Sharf, Lindbeck, Pargament, Shinran scholarship, Dogen practice-realization scholarship, upaya literature, and apophatic theology before raising novelty above extended.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.68 to about 0.56. The register-first discipline is strongly anticipated by comparative theology, hermeneutics, contextualist mysticism scholarship, Lindbeck's cultural-linguistic model, Sharf's critique of generic experience, Pargament's agency styles, Shinran's Other Power, Dogen's practice-realization, and MN 22's self-limiting doctrine use. The remaining contribution is a useful operational extension with predictive fields, not a discovery.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners and scholars say this is standard comparative theology, upaya, self-power versus Other Power, practice-realization, apophatic self-limitation, cultural-linguistic theology, or religious coping theory. They also say the rubric does not change teaching decisions or predict student failure modes.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners say the register-first rule prevents common distortions, and the vulnerability-safeguard fields predict real warning profiles, student errors, repair instructions, and points where analytic comparison must yield to practice.
questions: Does this rubric preserve your tradition's account of whose act transformation is, or does it still translate the tradition into alien agency categories?
Are the listed vulnerabilities real teaching concerns in your lineage, and which central vulnerabilities are missing?
Would this tool help predict student failure cases or teacher safeguards, or does it merely rename warnings already obvious to trained practitioners?
Where would your tradition require the analysis itself to stop, reverse, or be treated as part of the obstacle?
relevant_practitioners: Jodo Shinshu ministers and Shinran scholars
Soto Zen teachers familiar with Dogen's practice-realization
Dzogchen or Mahamudra teachers
Christian theologians of grace and apophatic practitioners
Sufi teachers or scholars of fana and baqa
Comparative theologians and religious-studies method scholars

primary_pair: Shinran, Tannisho, especially chapters 1, 6, 8, 10, and 11, trans. Taitetsu Unno, https://www.themathesontrust.org/papers/fareasternreligions/Tannisho-Unno.pdf
Dogen, Bendowa, especially sections 3, 4, 7, and 10, https://www.abuddhistlibrary.com/Buddhism/C%20-%20Zen/Ancestors/Dogen/Bendowa/Bendowa.htm
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model may turn ordinary comparative-theology caution into a new technical vocabulary. It may also smuggle in a practitioner-event boundary, even with a self-limitation marker, in traditions where the primary grammar is no-boundary, practice-realization, divine initiative, or nondual enactment.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The closest scholarly prior art includes Oliver Freiberger on comparison as selection, description, juxtaposition, redescription, rectification, and theory formation; Paul Hedges on comparative theology and hermeneutics; Francis Clooney's comparative theology; Katz and Sharf on contextualist and anti-experience-flattening method; Lindbeck's cultural-linguistic model; and Pargament's religious problem-solving styles. The audit did not fully close-read Clooney, Lindbeck, Dzogchen root texts, Meister Eckhart, Qushayri, Ibn Arabi, or Shinran's letters. That gap prevents any candidate-discovery claim.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: The Tannisho strongly supports the register-first move. Its decisive register is Other Power, and it names boundary failures such as self-power, calculation, doubt, wrong teacher ownership, and confusing nembutsu with the practitioner's own good act. Bendowa is the anomaly case. It names wrong understanding, false teachers, verbal clinging, and inadequate practice, but its register is practice-realization, where training and enlightenment are not two. The proposed model reads Shinran well, but its interface language strains Dogen because an interface can imply the very separation between practitioner, practice, and realization that Bendowa rejects.

title: The Register-Sensitive Path Interface
unlike_statement: Unlike Freiberger, Clooney, Hedges, Katz, Sharf, Lindbeck, Pargament, Shinran, Dogen, and MN 22, this idea claims that comparative work should use a specific two-layer rubric: preserve the transformation register first, then code only tradition-named vulnerabilities, safeguards, and warnings against the rubric itself. Its contribution is operational and predictive, not the discovery that traditions have different registers.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Dogen's Bendowa practice-realization, especially the claim that training and enlightenment are not two, https://www.abuddhistlibrary.com/Buddhism/C%20-%20Zen/Ancestors/Dogen/Bendowa/Bendowa.htm
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence in the model as a universal explanatory structure. It remains useful as a comparative heuristic, but the anomaly blocks promotion above novel_synthesis until Dogen, Pure Land, Dzogchen, and Christian grace cases are coded without distortion.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If practice and realization are one, then load-transfer language can create the separation Dogen rejects. Zazen is not simply practitioner agency carrying a burden, nor divine or vow relocation. It is enactment of realization. The ledger can handle this only by adding values such as no-transfer, no-boundary, and analyst-frame-refused. Those values may save accuracy but weaken explanatory compression.

claim_units: Capacity is vector-valued, not scalar.
The recurring roles are receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration.
Each role must be coded for magnitude, direction, locus, stage, safeguards, and failure mode.
Load can move from practitioner to teacher, vow, community, text, practice, or divine source only if a compatible capacity or reorientation is present.
The vector directions may be generated by whole-path soteriology rather than by local practice technique alone.
The model should predict cross-training resistance, student failure modes, and bridging practices better than scalar prerequisite models or broad agency-locus typologies.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Students or clients with equal baseline ability should show different dropout, alliance rupture, transfer, and relapse patterns depending on whether the method requires self-direction, surrender to process, external scaffolding, memory of rules, reflexive monitoring, or post-session integration. Bridging exercises that reorient the capacity should improve transfer more than simply increasing intensity.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Replace spiritual capacities with learning or therapeutic capacities: attention, agency, trust, memory, reflexivity, and integration. Code each by magnitude, direction, support locus, stage, safeguard, and failure mode. A change-oriented protocol, acceptance-oriented protocol, exposure protocol, relational repair protocol, and skills curriculum should fail differently when the learner has the wrong direction of capacity rather than merely too little capacity.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: General executive function, motivation, instructor quality, trauma history, or dosage predicts outcomes as well as or better than vector direction, and direction coding has low inter-rater reliability.

exact_claim: A spiritual path requires not only more or less practitioner capacity, but directionally specific capacities. Receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration should be coded as vector roles with magnitude, direction, locus, stage, safeguards, and failure mode. A path can lower or relocate a load only through a directionally compatible capacity or an explicit reorientation practice. The deeper generator of direction may be the path's account of self, liberation, and what remains after transformation.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Independent coders should infer later manual warnings and safeguards from early claims about self, liberation, and final transformation at above-chance rates. For example, Other Power texts should warn against calculation, practice-realization texts should warn against treating practice as a means to a later goal, and deconstructive paths should warn against reification and dissociation.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Capacity direction is generated by soteriology rather than only by practice technique.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind coding trial across SN 45.8, Tannisho, Bendowa, one Dzogchen or Mahamudra manual, one Christian grace manual, and one Sufi fana or baqa source.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Scalar maturity, broad tradition labels, or Pargament-style agency locus predict the same warnings as well as the vector ledger, or coders cannot agree on direction and locus.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Practitioners shifting from effort-heavy practice to surrender, vow, grace, or recognition practice should report specific misfires such as calculation, over-efforting, ownership of insight, or refusal of support. Teachers should prescribe bridging practices that change direction, not merely more practice time.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Reorientation cost is real and not just lack of general maturity.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Structured interviews with dual-trained practitioners in Theravada and Pure Land, Advaita and bhakti, Zen and Dzogchen or Mahamudra, plus teacher reports of student switching failures.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Experienced practitioners switch smoothly when motivation and discipline are high, with no direction-specific friction, or teachers describe failures only as intensity, trauma, poor ethics, or generic immaturity.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Adverse-practice reports should cluster by mismatch type: agency-heavy striving in surrender paths, deconstructive inquiry without integration capacity, devotional dependence without ethical agency, or nondual recognition without memory and stabilization supports.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Vector mismatch predicts adverse practice patterns better than broad practice category alone.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Recode VCE-style meditation challenge reports and practitioner case reports with vector fields, then compare predictive value against existing practice, practitioner, relationship, and context factors.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practice intensity, psychiatric history, retreat setting, sleep, teacher quality, or social context explain the reports without residual value from vector direction and support locus.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Jodo Shinshu, Dzogchen, Mahamudra, and Christian grace teachers should accept some functional preconditions while objecting to capacity ownership language. Their corrections should cluster around vow-held, teacher-held, grace-held, recognition-event, and analyst-frame-refused values.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Gift and recognition traditions still have observable conditions, but those conditions will not be best described as practitioner-owned capacities.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Practitioner-scholar interviews and manual coding of Shinran, pointing-out instructions, and grace texts.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Teachers either accept ordinary capacity language without reservation, or reject all functional precondition coding while still showing no predictable teaching safeguards.
