near_neighbors.difference: Pargament studies coping and divine-human problem solving, not a cross-tradition ledger of receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration across soteriologies.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The vector ledger must prove it predicts more than agency locus and divine involvement.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for locus and direction. Pargament already distinguishes self-directing, deferring, and collaborative religious problem-solving styles by the relationship between person and God, and ties those styles to competence and religiousness.
near_neighbors.source: Pargament et al., Religion and the Problem-Solving Process: Three Styles of Coping, https://icmglt.org/icmlibrary/religion-and-the-problem-solving-process-three-styles-of-coping/

near_neighbors.difference: Engler is mainly psychodynamic and Buddhist practice-facing. He does not supply a comparative vector ledger across grace, vow, nondual recognition, self-power, and practice-realization traditions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the capacity prerequisite claim. Remaining novelty is the multi-locus, multi-direction, cross-tradition rubric.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for prerequisite capacities, sequencing, and failure modes. Engler explicitly links meditation suitability to ego development, self-observation, affect tolerance, stability, and teacher choice of practice.
near_neighbors.source: Jack Engler, Therapeutic Aims in Psychotherapy and Meditation, https://www.atpweb.org/jtparchive/trps-16-84-01-025.pdf, and Inquiring Mind interview, https://inquiringmind.com/article/0501_1_engler-interview/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds receiving, agency, memory, support locus, safeguards, soteriological direction, and inter-tradition transfer costs.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for vector-like practice dimensions. Moderate remaining novelty for adding soteriological direction and load relocation.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Their attentional, constructive, and deconstructive families already treat practices as training different cognitive and affective mechanisms, with practice positions in a phenomenological matrix and resource bottlenecks.
near_neighbors.source: Dahl, Lutz, and Davidson, Reconstructing and Deconstructing the Self, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4595910/ and Investigating the Phenomenological Matrix, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4608430/

near_neighbors.difference: VCE is empirical and Buddhist meditation-focused, while the candidate proposes a comparative soteriological ledger for many kinds of path architecture.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction. The candidate should adopt VCE variables instead of reinventing adverse-practice coding.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for failure-mode prediction. The VCE study codes meditation-related challenges by practice factors, practitioner factors, relationships, context, and integration difficulties.
near_neighbors.source: Lindahl et al., The varieties of contemplative experience, PLOS ONE, https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0176239

near_neighbors.difference: Hadot concerns ancient philosophical schools and way-of-life formation, not cross-tradition soteriology or vector load transfer among practitioner, vow, teacher, community, or divine source.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The ledger extends a known spiritual-exercise account into a comparative coding tool.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for directed formation. Hadot describes practices of attention, memorization, self-mastery, therapy of passions, duties, and master-student guidance as exercises that re-habituate perception and life.
near_neighbors.source: Pierre Hadot, spiritual exercises, Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy, https://iep.utm.edu/hadot/

near_neighbors.difference: Ferrer gives a pluralist and participatory theory, not a cell-level rubric for capacities, safeguards, and failure modes.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction for the soteriology-level caution. The candidate is novel only if the rubric yields usable predictions.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for the warning that spiritual knowing is participatory, embodied, plural, and not reducible to a single perennial structure.
near_neighbors.source: Jorge Ferrer, The Plurality of Religions and the Spirit of Pluralism, https://digitalcommons.ciis.edu/ijts-transpersonalstudies/vol28/iss1/14/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate agrees with contextual caution but tries to operationalize it as vector coding rather than stop at critique.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the claim that direction comes from tradition. Remaining novelty is practical and predictive, not theoretical.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for anti-generic comparison. Katz and Sharf both pressure the assumption that contemplative states, reports, or capacities can be extracted from doctrinal, ritual, linguistic, and institutional settings.
near_neighbors.source: Robert Sharf, Buddhist Modernism and the Rhetoric of Meditative Experience, https://deepblue.lib.umich.edu/items/25b7e9d4-d1f3-4280-8d53-95fa0b53db5c, and Steven Katz contextualism discussion, https://www.mdpi.com/2077-1444/13/10/983

near_neighbors.difference: Upaya is intra-Buddhist pedagogy and teacher discernment. The vector ledger generalizes the structure across traditions and separates magnitude, direction, locus, stage, safeguards, and failure mode.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for adaptation-to-capacity. Moderate remaining novelty for the multi-axis comparative tool.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for matching instruction to practitioner capacity. Upaya already treats teachings as adapted to limited capacities and directed toward transformation.
near_neighbors.source: Buddhist upaya and capacity-adapted teaching, Harvard Divinity Bulletin, https://bulletin.hds.harvard.edu/buddhist-studies-the-buddhist-way/

near_neighbors.difference: Tannisho is one primary path grammar, not a comparative model. It may be better treated as an anomaly than as support.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction and high anomaly pressure. The candidate must avoid recoding gift as practitioner-owned capacity.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for load relocation and forbidden self-claiming. Tannisho explicitly denies that nembutsu is the practitioner's self-powered practice or good act.
near_neighbors.source: Shinran and Tannisho on Other Power, https://www.themathesontrust.org/papers/fareasternreligions/Tannisho-Unno.pdf and practitioner summary, https://tricycle.org/article/self-power-and-other-power/

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as a metaphysical claim. Treat it as a comparative and practitioner-facing coding heuristic.
Build the vector ledger as a coding sheet with fields for role, magnitude, direction, locus, stage, safeguard, failure mode, evidence, and analyst-frame refusal.
Add explicit values for practitioner-owned, teacher-held, vow-held, grace-held, community-held, text-held, practice-realization, recognition-event, no-transfer, and undecidable.
Close-read Shinran's Tannisho and letters, SN 45.8, Bendowa or Genjokoan, one Dzogchen or Mahamudra pointing-out manual, one Christian grace text, and one Sufi fana or baqa source before public use.
Test whether soteriology predicts capacity direction by coding self, liberation, and remainder claims before reading later warning sections.
Run an inter-rater trial comparing the vector ledger against scalar capacity, Pargament agency styles, and VCE-style practice, practitioner, relationship, and context variables.
Interview practitioners for concrete student failure cases and repair instructions, not abstract agreement with the model.
Do not raise above novel_synthesis unless the ledger predicts warnings, switching resistance, or adverse-practice patterns better than existing models.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.70 to about 0.61. The scalar-to-vector move is useful, but its parts are strongly anticipated by Pargament's agency styles, Engler's developmental prerequisites, Dahl and Lutz's meditation mechanism taxonomies, VCE adverse-effect variables, Hadot's spiritual exercises, Ferrer's participatory pluralism, Katz and Sharf's contextualism, Buddhist upaya, and Shinran's Other Power. The remaining contribution is a promising operational synthesis with testable predictions, not a discovered law of spiritual paths.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is standard upaya, religious coping, contemplative-development theory, spiritual bypassing caution, cultural-linguistic theology, or ordinary lineage pedagogy. They also say the ledger does not change teaching choices or predict failures better than existing teacher judgment.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can use the ledger to predict failure modes, identify hidden reorientation costs, name support locus more accurately, and avoid common cross-tradition mistranslations that older scalar or agency-locus models miss.
questions: Are receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration separable teaching loads in your tradition, or does that list impose an outside psychology?
Does directionality predict actual sequencing, warnings, and repair instructions for students, or is it obvious under older terms such as upaya, grace, self-power, Other Power, or ego development?
When practitioners move between effort, surrender, vow, recognition, and practice-realization paths, do they need a reorientation practice, or only more maturity and guidance?
Does calling trust, recognition, grace, or practice-realization a capacity distort the tradition's own grammar?
Can the rubric predict a concrete student failure case before you tell us the lineage's usual repair?
relevant_practitioners: Jodo Shinshu ministers and Shinran scholars
Theravada meditation teachers familiar with staged effort and right effort
Soto Zen teachers familiar with Dogen's practice-realization
Dzogchen or Mahamudra teachers who give or study pointing-out instructions
Christian theologians of grace and spiritual directors
Contemplative clinicians familiar with meditation-related adverse effects
Comparative theologians and religious-studies method scholars

primary_pair: SN 45.8, Magga-vibhanga Sutta, translated by Thanissaro Bhikkhu, https://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/sn/sn45/sn45.008.than.html
Tannisho, sections VII to IX, translated by Taitetsu Unno, https://www.themathesontrust.org/papers/fareasternreligions/Tannisho-Unno.pdf
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The ledger may turn tradition-specific soteriology into analyst-owned cells. Pure Land entrusting, Dogen practice-realization, and Dzogchen recognition may not be practitioner capacities at all, even when they have observable preconditions.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The audit searched Pargament, Engler, Hadot, Ferrer, Katz, Sharf, Dahl, Lutz, Davidson, Lindahl, Britton, upaya literature, Tannisho, Bendowa, and SN 45.8. It did not close-read Shinran's letters, Kyogyoshinsho, Longchenpa, Wangchuk Dorje, Christian grace manuals, Sufi fana and baqa texts, or original Pali and Japanese terms. That gap blocks any candidate-discovery claim.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 45.8 reads like a high-agency ledger: right view knows suffering and cessation, right resolve is directed toward renunciation and harmlessness, right effort generates desire and persistence, right mindfulness maintains ardent attention, and right concentration stabilizes the path. Tannisho VIII reverses the ownership grammar: nembutsu is not the practitioner's practice or good act because it is Other Power and free of self-power. Tannisho IX treats lack of joy not as simple incapacity but as blind passion already anticipated by the vow. The pair supports the vector insight because similar path functions are assigned different direction and locus. It also threatens the model because calling entrusting a capacity may already distort Shinran's grammar.

title: The Vector Capacity Ledger
unlike_statement: Unlike Pargament, Engler, Dahl, Hadot, Ferrer, Katz, Sharf, upaya theory, and Shinran, this idea claims that path requirements can be coded as a reusable vector ledger where each functional role has magnitude, direction, locus, stage, safeguards, and failure mode, and that whole-path soteriology may generate the direction of each role. Its possible contribution is predictive rubric design, not the discovery that traditions differ in agency, capacity, or context.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: No-threshold entry: inherited practice and Dogen's practice-realization grammar, especially Bendowa's claim that beginner practice is already original realization and that there is no beginning in practice.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: This lowers confidence substantially. The model should add no-threshold, inherited-practice, gradual-entry, and analyst-frame-refused values, and it should predict weakly unless a later recommitment, crisis, initiation, vow, or teacher-recognition event creates a second-order threshold.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If a path is entered by gradual socialization, inherited belonging, or a practice-realization grammar that refuses a boundary between practice and realization, then no discrete entry-state capacity may be identifiable. The ledger may become a total life-history description rather than a threshold model. It also becomes hard to distinguish grammar from institution, family, habit, and ordinary enculturation.

claim_units: Entry requires threshold capacities before conscious practice can begin.
Those capacities may be distributed across practitioner, teacher, community, text, ritual, crisis, vow, institution, or latent awareness.
Traditions apply a grammar of beginning that selects which threshold factor counts as decisive.
That grammar is not merely retrospective, it disciplines later practice through warning, trust, repetition, fear, and verification.
The better unit of comparison is not effort versus gift or first-break alone, but entry capacity locus plus post-threshold grammar.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Fields with similar technical content but different beginning grammars will produce different novice safeguards. Medicine, psychotherapy, law, aviation, and software incident response should differ in whether they warn mainly against overconfidence, boundary violation, protocol drift, emotional flooding, improvisation, or failure to escalate, even when entrants have similar ability.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: A training path begins when enough entry-state capacity is held by student, supervisor, cohort, institution, protocol, case exposure, crisis, licensure structure, and professional identity. The field's grammar of beginning then tells novices what to claim, trust, repeat, fear, and verify.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If novice safeguards are explained almost entirely by objective task risk and institutional liability, with no independent effect from professional beginning grammar, the entry-state structure does not travel well.

exact_claim: A path begins when enough entry-state capacity is held somewhere in the person-world field, then a tradition's grammar of beginning disciplines that threshold by reallocating what the practitioner may claim, trust, repeat, fear, and verify. The real comparative unit is the entry-state ledger: which capacities are required before conscious practice can begin, where those capacities are held, and which grammar disciplines them after the threshold.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: In paired communities with similar diaspora context, demographics, institutional density, and teacher access, Other Power or grace grammars will produce more warnings against self-powered calculation, more trust and receiving language, and different repair instructions than effort-centered traditions.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Entry grammar adds predictive value beyond social ecology.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind paired-case coding of Jodo Shinshu and Theravada, or evangelical and contemplative Catholic entry materials, in the same region.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: If safeguards and repairs track institution, teacher style, class, ethnicity, or convert versus cradle status better than coded grammar, the ledger loses explanatory force.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Coders using only entry texts can predict which later warnings emphasize pride, calculation, despair, antinomianism, passivity, premature certainty, lack of verification, or inadequate effort above chance.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The grammar can be coded from early entry texts and used to predict later manual warnings.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Split-source design: entry sermons, initiation liturgies, vows, or doctrinal entry passages coded first, later teacher manuals and interviews held out.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: If coders cannot predict warnings above chance, or predictions only work when the same texts are used for both coding and testing, the model is circular.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Within one tradition, sublineages or debates that share institution and culture but differ on grace, effort, vow, sudden awakening, or teacher-mediated recognition will show different threshold safeguards and different named entry failures.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Capacity locus and grammar are separable.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Augustinian versus Pelagian disputes, jiriki versus tariki debates, gradual versus sudden Chan, or Soto practice-realization versus koan-curriculum entry.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: If intra-tradition differences do not change safeguards once institution and social setting are held constant, grammar may be secondary rhetoric.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Failed entry cases will show missing or misassigned ledger functions: no trusted authority, weak community holding, unresolved crisis, mismatched language, unsupported memory practice, or forbidden self-claiming in a grammar that rejects self-power.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: A distributed entry ledger should help explain failed beginnings and dropout.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Retrospective interviews and prospective intake records for seekers entering meditation centers, churches, mosques, sanghas, Pure Land temples, or initiatory groups.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: If dropout is better predicted by generic social support, mental health, schedule burden, cost, or leader quality with no additional contribution from ledger variables, the model is mostly renamed sociology.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds a comparative coding claim: the mixed entry ecology is filtered by a doctrine-specific grammar that reallocates capacity and predicts later safeguards.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The entry ecology portion is mostly known. Remaining novelty depends on showing incremental prediction from grammar-coded capacity allocation.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for entry ecology. Rambo treats conversion as multifaceted change with personal, cultural, social, and religious dimensions, including context, crisis, encounter, learning roles, rituals, rhetoric, commitment, and consequences.
near_neighbors.source: Lewis R. Rambo, Understanding Religious Conversion, Yale University Press, https://yalebooks.yale.edu/book/9780300065152/understanding-religious-conversion/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate moves from coping to path entry and adds teacher, community, text, ritual, vow, institution, crisis, and latent awareness as possible support loci.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for agency-locus claims. Moderate remaining novelty if the ledger predicts entry safeguards beyond Pargament's styles.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for responsibility distribution. Pargament already distinguishes self-directing, deferring, and collaborative religious problem-solving styles by how agency is assigned between person and God.
near_neighbors.source: Kenneth Pargament et al., Religion and the Problem-Solving Process: Three Styles of Coping, https://icmglt.org/icmlibrary/religion-and-the-problem-solving-process-three-styles-of-coping/

near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction. Distributed entry is already well theorized outside religion.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for person-world entry. Learning begins through structured participation, discourse, identity formation, artifacts, and community resources, not isolated individual competence.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate focuses less on ritual sequence and more on which functional capacities are held by practitioner, vow, teacher, community, text, institution, or crisis, then predicts warnings and repairs.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. Threshold structure is known, but the ledger could add a sharper coding instrument.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for threshold, transition, ritual holding, and incorporation into a new state or identity.
near_neighbors.source: Arnold van Gennep and Victor Turner on rites of passage and liminality, overview source, https://www.britannica.com/science/rite-of-passage

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate generalizes capacity-sensitive guidance across traditions and separates entry locus, grammar, safeguard, and failure mode.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The idea should not claim discovery of capacity-sensitive entry. Its contribution must be comparative and predictive.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for matching instruction to capacity and for teacher-mediated threshold management. The Lotus Sutra presents varied means used to guide beings into the path.
near_neighbors.source: Buddhist upaya and Lotus Sutra expedient means, Chapter 2 translation page, https://xuanfa.net/buddha-dharma/tripitaka/sutras/lotus-sutra/chapter-2-expedient-means/

near_neighbors.difference: Shinran gives one path grammar, not a cross-tradition predictive rubric.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction and high anomaly pressure. The model must avoid recoding Other Power as an analyst-owned capacity.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for forbidden self-claiming and load relocation. Shinran explicitly treats Other Power as decisive and denies that nembutsu is self-power practice.
near_neighbors.source: Shinran, Chapter on Practice, Collected Works of Shinran, https://shinranworks.com/the-major-expositions/chapter-on-practice/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate narrows grammar to the beginning of a path and ties it to a concrete ledger of entry capacities and safeguards.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the grammar language. Remaining novelty is the entry-specific operational rubric.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for the claim that religious systems function like languages and doctrines regulate practice and experience like grammar.
near_neighbors.source: George Lindbeck, cultural-linguistic theory of religion and doctrine as grammar, summarized in Boston Collaborative Encyclopedia of Western Theology, https://people.bu.edu/wwildman/bce/mwt_themes_862_lindbeck.htm

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate begins earlier than meditation suitability and includes vow-held, grace-held, ritual-held, text-held, and institution-held entry conditions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction. The prerequisite-capacity claim is not new, but the pre-practice cross-tradition ledger may extend it.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for prerequisite capacities, teacher judgment, practice suitability, and failure modes before meditation practice.
near_neighbors.source: Jack Engler, Therapeutic Aims in Psychotherapy and Meditation, https://www.atpweb.org/jtparchive/trps-16-84-01-025.pdf

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds tradition-specific authority, vow, ritual, grace, and teacher-held capacity, and asks for manual-level safeguards.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. Crisis-threshold and frame-change claims are known.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for crisis or disorientation as an entry condition that leads to changed frames, reflection, and changed practice.
near_neighbors.source: Jack Mezirow's transformative learning theory and disorienting dilemma, overview source, https://pressbooks.nebraska.edu/onlineteaching/chapter/transformative-learning-mezirow/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate is more focused on threshold comparison and predictive safeguards, while socialization theory often weakens the idea that entry has a discrete beginning.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High anomaly pressure. Inherited practice may force the model to include no-threshold and gradual-entry cases.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for inherited and gradual incorporation into religious identity through family, institution, ritual, and community from birth onward.
near_neighbors.source: Religious socialization literature, Encyclopedia.com overview, https://www.encyclopedia.com/religion/encyclopedias-almanacs-transcripts-and-maps/socialization-religious

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as a metaphysical account of how paths really begin. Treat it as an interpretive and practitioner-facing coding heuristic.
Build a split-source rubric: code entry grammar only from entry texts, liturgies, sermons, vows, or initiation instructions, then predict safeguards from separate manuals and interviews.

Add rubric values for no-threshold, inherited practice, gradual socialization, recommitment threshold, crisis threshold, teacher-recognition threshold, vow-held threshold, grace-held threshold, practice-realization, and analyst-frame-refused.
Separate entry ecology from grammar by coding social context, institution, teacher contact, family inheritance, crisis, language access, ritual access, and trust before coding doctrine.

Run intra-tradition tests where institution and culture are similar but beginning grammar differs, such as Augustinian and Pelagian disputes, jiriki and tariki debates, sudden and gradual Chan, or Soto and Rinzai entry pedagogy.
Use inherited-practice and Dogen-style no-beginning cases as anomaly tests rather than marginal examples.
Ask practitioners for concrete failed-beginning cases and repair instructions, not abstract agreement with the model.

Do not raise above novel_synthesis unless split-source blind coding predicts safeguards or failure modes better than conversion studies, Pargament agency styles, upaya, liminality, situated-learning, and religious-socialization variables.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.82 to about 0.64. The distributed-entry and threshold pieces are strongly anticipated by Rambo's conversion model, liminality, situated learning, religious socialization, upaya, Pargament's agency styles, Engler's prerequisites, Lindbeck's cultural-linguistic grammar, and Shinran's Other Power. The remaining contribution is a promising operational synthesis: a split-source predictive rubric linking entry capacity loci to tradition-specific warning and repair patterns.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say the model is just conversion studies, upaya, religious coping, catechesis, initiation theory, lineage pedagogy, or common pastoral judgment. They cannot use it to predict failures, and they object that the ledger misnames grace, vow, inherited belonging, or teacher recognition as capacities.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can use the ledger to distinguish two beginners who look socially similar but require different supports, to name hidden threshold loads, to predict common failures before they occur, and to explain why entry instructions from one path can harm or confuse beginners in another.
questions: When a person begins in your tradition, which parts of beginning must already be held by the person, teacher, community, text, ritual, vow, grace, institution, crisis, or latent awareness?
Does your tradition's account of beginning predict concrete student mistakes and repair instructions, or is that only ordinary teacher judgment?
Would this ledger change how you screen, welcome, warn, sequence, or verify beginners?
Does calling entrusting, grace, recognition, inherited belonging, or practice-realization a capacity distort your tradition's own language?
Can you name one failed beginning that this rubric predicts before you explain the usual lineage repair?

Theravada meditation teachers
Christian spiritual directors and theologians of grace
Soto Zen and Chan teachers
Dzogchen or Mahamudra teachers familiar with pointing-out
Sufi teachers familiar with bayah, adab, fana, and baqa
Religious studies scholars of conversion and initiation
Contemplative clinicians familiar with adverse practice outcomes

primary_pair: SN 45.8, Magga-vibhanga Sutta, translated by Thanissaro Bhikkhu, Access to Insight, https://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/sn/sn45/sn45.008.than.html

Shinran, Chapter on Practice, Collected Works of Shinran, plus Tannisho, Matheson Trust page, https://shinranworks.com/the-major-expositions/chapter-on-practice/ and https://www.themathesontrust.org/library/tannisho-shin-buddhist-aphorisms
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model may impose systems vocabulary on traditions whose entry grammar is theological, ritual, inherited, communal, or explicitly anti-analytical. It also risks circularity if entry texts define the grammar and the same tradition's safeguards are then counted as successful predictions.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The audit searched conversion theory, religious coping, legitimate peripheral participation, liminality, upaya, transformative learning, religious socialization, Lindbeck's cultural-linguistic model, Shinran, SN 45.8, Dogen, Augustine, and the Council of Orange. It did not sufficiently close-read Kyogyoshinsho beyond the Chapter on Practice, Augustine and Pelagius in full primary-text sequence, Longchenpa or Mahamudra pointing-out manuals, Sufi bayah and fana texts, or original-language terms for entrusting, effort, vow, grace, and capacity. That gap blocks a candidate-discovery claim.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 45.8 makes the path legible through cultivated factors: right view, right resolve, right effort, right mindfulness, and right concentration are named as functions a practitioner develops. Shinran's Chapter on Practice reverses the ownership grammar: nembutsu is not self-power practice, Other Power is the decisive cause, and the Name or Primal Vow carries the decisive function. This supports the ledger's contrast because similar threshold functions are allocated to different loci. It also threatens the model because Pure Land sources may reject the analyst's capacity language rather than merely move capacity from practitioner to vow.

title: The Entry-State Ledger
unlike_statement: Unlike conversion-process theory, religious coping theory, upaya pedagogy, situated learning, liminality theory, Lindbeck's doctrine-as-grammar model, Engler's capacity prerequisites, and Shinran's Other Power grammar, this idea claims that path entry can be coded as a pre-practice distributed ledger plus a normative beginning grammar, and that this pairing should predict later safeguards, warnings, verification practices, and failure modes better than either entry ecology or doctrine alone.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Inherited practice and Dogen-style practice-realization cases, especially cradle religious formation and Soto Zen's refusal to separate practice from realization.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence from candidate-discovery range to extended or renamed range until the rubric includes no-threshold, gradual-socialization, practice-realization, and analyst-frame-refused values, then survives blind coding.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: Inherited formation may have no discrete first-break moment, and practice-realization may refuse the sequence of entry, support transfer, claimant prohibition, safeguard, and later verification. In these cases, the model's entry grammar may be an analyst artifact rather than a native structure.

claim_units: Traditions encode a grammar of beginning rather than only an entry doctrine or a forbidden claimant rule.
That grammar distributes decisive support across practitioner, teacher, community, ritual, vow, grace, latent capacity, family formation, or later authorization.
The grammar forbids or permits specific agents to claim decisive functions.
The grammar predicts dangers, safeguards, verification rules, and repair practices.
First-break doctrine may drive practice architecture in some traditions but may be secondary within a wider entry ecology in others.
A valid test must separate entry mechanism, claimant rule, anthropology, safeguard, and verification fields before claiming prediction.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Modalities that assign decisive change to client agency, therapist relationship, protocol fidelity, insight, nervous-system regulation, medication, group support, or acceptance should show different early dropout patterns, compliance failures, shame profiles, dependency risks, and repair scripts. A split-source coding of intake language should predict later troubleshooting guidance better than generic modality labels alone.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Entry-claimant grammar becomes intake-claimant grammar: a helping system specifies how change begins, who may own decisive agency, what support is externalized or internalized, what dangers follow, and how progress is verified.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If dropout and repair patterns are predicted better by severity, alliance, cost, access, therapist skill, or diagnosis than by intake-claimant grammar, the structure may be religion-specific or too weak to travel.

exact_claim: Transformative traditions teach an entry-claimant grammar: a linked pattern that explains how beginning is possible, where necessary support is held, which agent may or may not claim decisive functions, what dangers follow, and how teachers or communities verify the path. First-break doctrine is sometimes a driver of practice architecture, but sometimes only one node in a wider ecology of family formation, crisis, teacher encounter, ritual repetition, latent capacity, and later authorization. Any predictive use must first test whether entry doctrine can be isolated from claimant language, anthropology, safeguards, and verification rules without construct leakage.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Blind coders using only entry texts can distinguish entry mechanism from forbidden claimant, and those separate codes predict different warning and verification patterns in later manuals with above-chance agreement.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Entry doctrine and claimant prohibition are separable variables in at least some traditions.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Split-source manual coding across Shinran Pure Land, Theravada path-factor texts, Orange or Augustinian grace texts, Dogen practice-realization, and Dzogchen or Mahamudra pointing-out manuals.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Coders cannot separate entry mechanism from claimant rule, or later safeguards can be predicted equally well from generic tradition labels, conversion-stage theory, religious socialization, or Pargament agency styles.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Texts and teachers in Other Power, grace, and surrender-centered traditions repeatedly warn against predictable misuse of non-self-efficacy language, and these warnings differ from effort-centered path warnings.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Strong anti-self-claimant traditions should generate distinctive safeguards against moral license, despair, counterfeit passivity, or self-power contamination.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Primary manuals, sermons, letters, and practitioner interviews in Jodo Shinshu, Christian grace traditions, bhakti or prapatti, and Sufi tawakkul.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Warnings are not distinctive, are mostly generic moral warnings, or are better predicted by institutional history, teacher style, sectarian polemic, or social setting than by claimant grammar.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Practitioners moving between effort-centered, grace-centered, teacher-recognition-centered, and practice-realization paths report confusion about who or what is allowed to own progress, failure, verification, or decisive transformation.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Hybrid or cross-training friction should often occur at claimant boundaries, not only at doctrine or technique boundaries.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Structured interviews with dual-trained practitioners and teachers, coded independently from doctrinal affiliation.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Reported friction clusters around vocabulary, social identity, ritual preference, or teacher relationship without identifiable claimant-boundary conflicts.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Cradle religion, family-formation cases, and gradual socialization show lower separability between entry doctrine and safeguard architecture than conversion, initiation, vow, grace, or pointing-out cases.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Where entry ecology is dominant and doctrine is weakly foregrounded, claimant grammar should have lower predictive power.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Comparison of inherited-practice communities, adult convert settings, monastic initiation settings, and recommitment movements within the same broad tradition.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Claimant grammar predicts equally well everywhere, suggesting the model is too elastic, or fails everywhere after controlling for socialization and institutional variables.

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as a new doctrine candidate. Treat it as a consolidation and extension of Entry-State Ledger, Forbidden Claimant Rubric, Credit Distribution, and First-Break Problem.
Build a split-source coding protocol with separate fields for entry mechanism, support locus, forbidden claimant, anthropology, safeguard, verification rule, failure mode, and analyst-frame refusal.
Require coders to code entry grammar from entry texts only, then predict safeguards from separate manuals or interviews. Do not let the same passage count as both cause and prediction.

Add values for no-threshold, inherited socialization, gradual recommitment, crisis threshold, vow-held entry, grace-held entry, teacher-recognition entry, practice-realization, no-transfer, collapsed-transfer, and analyst-frame-refused.

Run a comparison against baseline models: Rambo conversion stages, Pargament agency styles, Lindbeck doctrine-as-grammar, upaya capacity pedagogy, liminality, situated learning, religious socialization, and prior Lumenary capacity ledger.
Use Dogen practice-realization, inherited religion, and successful cross-training as anomaly tests, not as supporting examples.
Interview practitioners for actual failed-beginning cases and repair instructions rather than asking whether they agree with the model.
Do not raise above extended unless blind coding shows that entry-claimant grammar predicts warnings, verification practices, or failure repairs better than existing models and prior Lumenary rubrics.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.72 to about 0.58. The idea is not a discovery-level concept. Its entry ecology is strongly anticipated by Rambo, Berger, liminality, situated learning, and religious socialization. Its grammar language is anticipated by Lindbeck. Its agency distribution is anticipated by Pargament and grace or Other Power sources. Its support-locus and safeguard rubric is very close to prior Lumenary Entry-State Ledger, Capacity Ledger, Forbidden Claimant, and Credit Distribution records. The remaining contribution is an extension: an independence audit that tries to prevent construct leakage between entry doctrine, claimant rule, anthropology, safeguard, and verification.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is obvious pastoral or lineage judgment, ordinary conversion theory, upaya, catechesis, initiation theory, grace theology, religious coping, religious socialization, or the existing Lumenary capacity ledger. They cannot identify any new prediction, and they object that claimant grammar distorts traditions that reject agency accounting.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can use the fields to predict concrete beginner errors, distinguish otherwise similar entry cases, avoid harmful transfer of instructions across paths, and name safeguards that are not captured by conversion stages, effort versus grace language, or generic teacher judgment.
questions: When someone begins in your path, who or what is allowed to claim the decisive beginning: practitioner, teacher, community, ritual, vow, grace, practice itself, latent nature, or no claimant?
Do the most common beginner failures in your tradition follow from a wrong claimant, a wrong entry condition, ordinary immaturity, or social context?
Could you use this grammar to predict a safeguard or repair instruction before consulting your manuals, or is it only a new label for knowledge you already use?
Does the language of support locus, claimant, and verification distort your tradition's own terms for faith, entrusting, practice, grace, recognition, or realization?
Can you name one failed beginning where this rubric would change screening, instruction, sequencing, or repair?

primary_pair: SN 45.8, Magga-vibhanga Sutta, translated by Thanissaro Bhikkhu, Access to Insight, https://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/sn/sn45/sn45.008.than.html
Tannisho sections 1, 6, and 8, translation by Taitetsu Unno, https://nembutsu.cc/buddhism/the-tannisho/tannisho-sections-i-to-x/
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The phrase grammar risks renaming Lindbeck, Rambo, Pargament, Berger, religious socialization theory, and Lumenary's own Entry-State Ledger unless the candidate produces non-circular coding fields and predictive wins. It also risks imposing analyst-owned agency language on traditions that explicitly refuse the claimant frame.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The audit searched conversion studies, doctrine-as-grammar theory, religious coping agency styles, sociology of plausibility structures, liminality, situated learning, upaya, Shinran Other Power, Orange grace canons, Dogen practice-realization, religious socialization, and prior Lumenary capacity-ledger audits. It did not fully close-read Augustine against Pelagius, Longchenpa or Mahamudra pointing-out manuals, Sufi bayah and fana texts, Sri Vaishnava prapatti sources, or original-language terms for faith, entrusting, effort, capacity, grace, and agency. That gap blocks a candidate-discovery claim.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 45.8 parses the path through practitioner-cultivated functions: right view, resolve, speech, action, livelihood, effort, mindfulness, and concentration. The grammar permits the practitioner to perform decisive training functions, though under Buddhist doctrinal constraints. Tannisho shifts decisive efficacy away from self-power and toward entrusting, Other Power, and the Primal Vow, while also warning against claiming disciples or treating nembutsu as self-owned practice or merit. This supports the candidate's entry-claimant link. It also shows the main risk: the same Shinran material supplies entry grammar, claimant prohibition, danger, and verification language at once, so a prediction test can easily become circular.

title: The Entry-Claimant Grammar
unlike_statement: Unlike Rambo's conversion-process model, Lindbeck's doctrine-as-grammar model, Pargament's religious agency styles, Berger's plausibility structures, upaya pedagogy, liminality theory, Shinran's Other Power grammar, Orange grace theology, religious socialization theory, and Lumenary's Entry-State Ledger, this idea claims that entry mechanism and forbidden claimant form one testable agency grammar, and that a split-source independence audit can determine whether that grammar predicts safeguards and verification practices without circularly reusing the same text as both cause and evidence.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Inherited practice and Dogen-style practice-realization, where there may be no discrete beginning, no transfer point, and no allowed separation between method and fulfillment.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence from discovery-level to extension-level. The model needs explicit values for no-threshold, practice-realization, inherited participation, continuous authorization, and analyst-frame-refused.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If a tradition treats practice itself as realization or treats participation as inherited social life rather than a chosen threshold, first-break grammar and handoff density may be analyst-imposed. The model may still code affordances and institutions, but its central authorization claim weakens.

claim_units: Beginning is not a single event but a layered process.
Family, crisis, ritual, teacher trust, imitation, community, and bodily practice function as empirical affordances that make participation possible.
Institutions select which affordances matter and how intensely they are applied.
A tradition's first-break grammar regulates which agency may be praised, corrected, disowned, suspended, or confirmed.
Repeated thresholds should be coded for scaffolding, transfer, repair, and reauthorization, not only the initial conversion moment.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Therapies with different authorization grammars should produce different beginner warnings and repair scripts even for similar symptoms: cognitive therapies should warn against passive insight without practice, psychodynamic therapies against premature technique ownership or enactment, and medication-centered care against misattributing all change to willpower.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Client change begins through empirical affordances, such as crisis and referral; institutional selection, such as clinic intake and modality; authorization grammar, such as whether insight belongs to client, therapist, alliance, medication, or protocol; and repeated handoffs, such as homework, exposure, relapse repair, and termination.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Repair scripts and dropout patterns are explained as well by diagnosis, alliance quality, socioeconomic access, clinician skill, and modality intensity, with no independent effect from authorization grammar or handoff density.

exact_claim: Transformative practice begins through layered authorization: empirical affordances make participation possible, institutions select and intensify those affordances, first-break grammar authorizes or forbids agency claims, and handoff density tracks repeated thresholds where capacity is scaffolded, claimed, forbidden, transferred, repaired, or reauthorized.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Blind coders using only entry texts should predict later warning profiles, such as pride warnings in self-effort paths, passivity warnings in grace or Other Power paths, and teacher-dependence warnings in recognition-based paths, above baseline models.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Entry grammar can be coded from doctrinal or initiation texts independently of pastoral manuals.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Split-corpus coding across Zen, Jodo Shinshu, Calvinist, Wesleyan, Dzogchen, Gelug, and Sufi entry materials.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Warnings are predicted equally well or better by institutional role, teacher style, social context, or conversion-stage variables, with no added value from agency authorization grammar.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Traditions with more repeated transfer or reauthorization points should show more explicit correction episodes, verification rituals, vows, teacher confirmations, or repair scripts than traditions with low handoff density.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Handoff density is a real variable rather than a retrospective label.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Manual coding of sermons, training manuals, interviews, and failure narratives separated from entry texts.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Correction and repair frequency tracks institution size, textual genre, monastic discipline, or teacher personality instead of handoff density.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: The same affordance, such as crisis, family inheritance, teacher trust, or bodily discipline, will be praised in one tradition, downgraded in another, and treated as dangerous in a third according to that tradition's entry grammar.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Institutional selection mediates which empirical affordances count as legitimate entry supports.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Paired traditions in similar social contexts but different doctrinal entry grammars.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Affordance valuation is mostly explained by local culture, class, literacy, recruitment context, or institutional survival needs rather than doctrinal authorization.

primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model combines many known variables and can become a retrospective container. It also risks circularity if entry grammar, institutional role, warnings, repair, and verification are coded from the same passage.

primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: Tannisho layers beginning through entrusting, Amida's Primal Vow, teacher humility, and a refusal to claim disciples or self-owned merit. SN 45.8 layers the path through trainable functions: view, resolve, speech, action, livelihood, effort, mindfulness, and concentration. The contrast supports the candidate's agency-authorization intuition, but it also shows that the strongest pieces are already visible in primary texts and prior Lumenary entry-claimant work.

title: Layered Authorization of Beginning
unlike_statement: Unlike Rambo's conversion stages, Lindbeck's doctrine-as-grammar, Pargament's religious agency styles, Lave and Wenger's situated learning, liminality theory, scaffolding theory, Shinran's Other Power grammar, and prior Lumenary entry-claimant work, this idea says beginning should be coded as four separable layers: empirical affordance, institutional selection, normative agency authorization, and repeated handoff density. Its possible contribution is not that beginnings are layered, but that these layers can be split into independent corpora and tested for prediction of warnings, repair instructions, and reauthorization episodes.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Longchen Rabjam's Trekcho instruction combines primordial, spontaneously present awareness with repeated instructions to meditate, observe karmic law, keep vows, cultivate devotion, and apply practice until self-grasping fades.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence and blocks candidate-discovery status. The model remains useful as a coding heuristic, but the stance categories need multi-value coding, stage coding, and an analyst-frame-refused option.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: This is not neatly regulatory, dissolutive, or ontological. It may be simultaneous rather than mixed-by-stage. If one primary text requires multiple stance codes at the same time, the model's categories may be heuristic tags rather than stable structural types.

claim_units: Support locus can be coded by function: receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, integration, teacher, vow, text, community, present act, awareness itself, or later integration.
The forbidden-claimant column is valid only where a tradition uses a regulatory grammar of identifiable agents, permissions, and prohibitions.
Dissolutive traditions do not merely prohibit appropriation, they unsettle the agent-action or subject-object frame that makes appropriation intelligible.
Ontological traditions deny that the decisive reality is produced, acquired, or missing, so practice removes obstruction rather than creating the goal.
Mixed-by-stage traditions shift claimability logic across phases, for example sudden awakening followed by gradual cultivation.
Stance should predict distinct failure modes: wrong claimant, formalism, reification, premature realization, passivity, or stage-transition confusion.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Psychotherapy and coaching models of client agency
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Therapies with similar techniques but different agency-frame stance should produce different failure modes. Agency-strengthening models should risk self-blame, surrender-based models should risk passivity, self-dissolving models should risk dissociation or avoidance, and already-whole models should risk bypassing concrete behavioral repair.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Support locus becomes the location of change functions: client, therapist, method, group, medication, environment, insight, or later integration. Claimability stance becomes the model's prior relation to agency: strengthen agency, share agency, dissolve a self-story, treat health as already present but obstructed, or shift stance by stage.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Failure patterns are explained by alliance, diagnosis, therapist skill, treatment dose, socioeconomic pressure, or trauma history with no additional predictive value from agency-frame stance.

exact_claim: A comparative practice rubric should first ask how a tradition relates to claim-making itself. Support locus and forbidden claimant are useful only after coding the tradition's claimability stance as regulatory, dissolutive, ontological, or mixed-by-stage. That prior stance should predict the tradition's characteristic safeguards and failure modes.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: If coders classify a text as dissolutive from doctrinal passages alone, later manual warnings should target attainment-reification, subject-object fixation, or formalism more than wrong effort level or wrong credit assignment.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Claimability stance predicts warning and repair patterns beyond credit distribution and support locus.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Split-source blind coding of doctrinal passages, then separate warning sections and teacher interviews.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Warnings are generic across traditions or are better predicted by teacher style, institution, intensity of practice, trauma risk, or ordinary upaya pedagogy.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Srivaishnava sub-schools should share a broadly regulatory claimability stance while differing on how effort and grace are distributed.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Claimability stance and credit distribution are separable variables.

falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: The stance changes whenever credit distribution changes, making the new variable redundant.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Advaita and Dzogchen teachers should report recurring cases where students use already-present language to avoid preparation, conduct, integration, or verification.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Ontological denial produces a distinctive premature-realization failure mode.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Practitioner interviews, adverse-practice reports, and comparison with spiritual-bypassing literature.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: These cases are no more common than in regulatory traditions, or spiritual bypassing explains them without reference to ontological claimability.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: In Chinul-like or Zen training contexts, confusion should cluster around the movement from recognition or sudden awakening into gradual cultivation and integration.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Mixed-by-stage transitions are high-vulnerability points.

falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Confusion clusters around retreat intensity, social isolation, teacher misconduct, or mental health factors rather than stance transitions.

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as doctrine. Treat it as a comparative and practitioner-facing coding heuristic.
Build a coding sheet with fields for function, support locus, forbidden claimant, claimability stance, stage, safeguard, failure mode, evidence passage, analyst-frame refusal, and confidence.

Do not raise above novel_synthesis unless blind coding shows that claimability stance predicts safeguards, repairs, or failure modes better than existing models and prior Lumenary ledgers.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.68 to about 0.60. The idea is not a discovery of anti-appropriation, non-doership, practice-realization, already-attained realization, grace-effort distribution, or stage-dependent cultivation. Those are well anticipated by primary texts, Lindbeck, Pargament, upaya pedagogy, and prior Lumenary records. The remaining contribution is a novel synthesis: claimability stance as a prior validity check for support-locus and forbidden-claimant coding.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is ordinary doctrine-as-grammar, religious coping, upaya, grace-effort theology, nondual pedagogy, spiritual bypassing, or prior Lumenary ledger language. Dissolutive and ontological teachers reject the rubric as reifying. Teachers cannot produce stance-specific repairs beyond familiar cautions against pride, laziness, premature claims, or poor integration.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners independently accept the stance distinction, code it without major distortion, and use it to predict concrete safeguards, repair instructions, screening decisions, or transition risks that support locus and credit distribution alone miss.

Does this rubric predict a concrete warning or repair before consulting a manual, or does it rename familiar upaya, grace, agency, or lineage pedagogy?
Can you describe one student failure case that this rubric explains better than ordinary effort, faith, morality, trauma, teacher fit, or community support?

primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The rubric improves the earlier forbidden-claimant model, but claimability stance may still be an analyst's frame. The mixed-by-stage category could absorb counterexamples unless limiting cases are defined. The model also risks coding support locus in traditions where separating support, actor, action, and realization is itself part of the error being criticized.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Search reached Pargament on religious problem-solving styles, Lindbeck on doctrine as rule or grammar, Dogen practice-realization sources, Longchen Rabjam Trekcho, Chinul sudden awakening and gradual cultivation, Srivaishnava prapatti debates, Gita action-doership passages, Shinran Other Power, and upaya literature. It did not fully close-read Lindbeck's full book, Pargament's full 1988 article, Tibetan commentarial material on direct introduction, Sanskrit Advaita sources on praptasya prapti, or Srivaishnava primary polemics. This blocks candidate-discovery status.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: MMK 8 attacks any independently established agent-action structure. Bendowa collapses a simple before-after relation between practice and realization, while still insisting on concrete sitting, teacher transmission, and trust. Together they support the candidate's warning that prohibition language can distort dissolutive traditions. They also reduce novelty, because the anti-appropriation move is primary-textual rather than newly discovered. The possible contribution is the comparative coding rule: do not use forbidden claimant until the tradition's claimability grammar has been identified.

title: The Claimability and Support Rubric
unlike_statement: Unlike Lindbeck's doctrine-as-grammar, Pargament's religious agency styles, Nagarjuna's agent-action critique, Dogen's practice-realization, Dzogchen primordial purity, Advaita praptasya prapti, Srivaishnava effort-grace debates, upaya pedagogy, and prior Lumenary credit-distribution records, this idea says the first coding question is whether the claiming framework itself is authorized, dissolved, ontologically denied, or stage-dependent. Its possible contribution is not the anti-appropriation insight, but the rule that support-locus and forbidden-claimant rubrics are valid only after that prior stance is coded.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Successful dual-trained practitioners who move between traditions with different final aims without predictable friction, or switchers whose failures track teacher fit, trauma history, retreat intensity, or technique mismatch rather than prior telos.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Keeps the idea below candidate discovery. The anomaly search lowers confidence in grain as a real independent variable, but supports the stricter surplus test as a useful guardrail.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If difficulty follows ordinary transfer distance, social ecology, or clinical risk factors, then telos-bearing residue adds no explanatory power. If incompatible teloi coexist smoothly, grain may be an artifact of poor pedagogy or identity conflict.

claim_units: Contemplative skills may carry directional residue from a prior path's final aim.
Procedural inertia is a rival explanation and must be controlled before invoking grain.
Claimant or transfer grammar is a second rival explanation and must be controlled before invoking grain.
Cross-training difficulty can be decomposed into procedural residue, claimant-grammar conflict, and telos-bearing residue.
Grain is the surplus directional residue left after ordinary skill transfer and practice ecology are accounted for.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Professional retraining, such as a litigator becoming a mediator or a classical musician becoming a jazz improviser.
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Retraining failures will be best explained by old success criterion only after task habit and institutional role grammar are controlled. The former litigator should struggle not only from adversarial tactics but from a residual win condition that organizes attention even after mediation procedures are learned.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Old dissolution target becomes old success criterion. Procedural residue becomes practiced operation. Claimant grammar becomes the new field's rules for who may act, decide, claim competence, or count success.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If retraining difficulty is fully explained by task habits, incentives, identity, and organizational norms, with no residual effect from old success criteria, the surplus-grain structure weakens.

exact_claim: A contemplative capacity should be called grained only when the prior dissolution target predicts transfer difficulty better than repeated operation and the receiving practice's transfer grammar. Cross-training failures should be coded as procedural residue, claimant-grammar conflict, and telos-bearing residue.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Blind coders can code a practitioner's prior telos from manuals or teacher interviews, then predict specific later transfer frictions beyond technique similarity and agency grammar.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Prior dissolution target has independent predictive value after operation and grammar are controlled.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Mixed-method cross-training study with preregistered coding and qualitative comparative analysis.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Old telos has no independent effect once task similarity, teacher relation, social identity, dosage, mental health, and receiving doctrine are included.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Same-technique different-grammar cases produce different failures than different-technique same-grammar cases, and telos-shift cases show an additional asymmetric pattern.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Procedural residue, claimant-grammar conflict, and telos-bearing residue are separable layers.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Anonymized practitioner narratives coded by independent contemplative teachers and researchers.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Inter-rater reliability fails, or all reported failures collapse into familiar categories such as habit, confusion, teacher mismatch, or motivation.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Onboarding that explicitly reframes final aim, ownership, verification, and repair reduces predictable switching errors more than technical instruction alone.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Retargeting a practice requires more than teaching the new operation.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Prospective comparison of cross-training onboarding protocols.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Telos reframing adds no improvement beyond rapport, safety screening, practice dosage, and community support.

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as doctrine. Treat as a research-control rule for cross-training studies.

Build a preregistered coding sheet for old operation, old dissolution target, receiving claimant grammar, teacher relation, community ecology, identity investment, mental health factors, and failure mode.
Use split-source blind coding: code prior telos from old manuals before reading outcomes, then code receiving grammar from new manuals before reading repairs.
Compare predictive value against transfer theory, situated learning, Hadot, Katz, Sharf, Lindbeck, Pargament, upaya, VCE, and prior Lumenary ledgers.
Use successful dual practice, inherited religion, radical other-power, and traditions that reject transfer accounting as anomaly classes.

novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.69 to about 0.62. Procedural transfer, practice ecology, doctrine as grammar, agency allocation, tradition-conditioned experience, and capacity-sensitive pedagogy are well anticipated. The remaining contribution is a stricter residual test for prior Lumenary grain claims.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is ordinary skill transfer, lineage pedagogy, upaya, spiritual direction, trauma-informed screening, or religious coping. They cannot identify telos-specific repairs beyond familiar cautions about pride, impatience, confusion, or poor fit.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners independently report cases where identical techniques fail differently because the student's previous final aim changes what they think the practice is doing, and they can name repairs that specifically retarget final aim rather than only technique or agency language.
questions: When students arrive from another tradition, can you distinguish old technique habits from old ownership or authority grammar?
Do some students bring a prior final aim that bends how they understand your practice even after learning the correct technique?
Can you give one concrete failure case where telos explains the problem better than technique, teacher fit, trauma, temperament, or community mismatch?
Does this three-layer model change how you repair the student's practice?
Are there traditions or students for whom the transfer framing itself distorts the situation?

primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. Telos-bearing residue may be a residual bucket for variables already covered by technique, teacher relation, community, social identity, trauma history, motivation, and doctrinal language. The model needs strict stopping rules so unexplained difficulty is not automatically called grain.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The search found strong near-neighbors in transfer-of-training theory, Barnett and Ceci's far-transfer taxonomy, Hadot on school-shaped spiritual exercises, Katz on tradition-conditioned experience, Lindbeck on doctrine as grammar, Pargament on agency allocation, situated learning, Buddhist upaya, and VCE adverse-effects coding. It did not yet close-read full books by Hadot, Katz, Sharf, Lindbeck, Lave and Wenger, or Pargament, and it did not code actual cross-training cases.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 45.8 presents path factors as trainable functions, including right view, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion. Dogen's Bendowa makes zazen not merely one transferable technique but the expression of the Way itself, where practice and realization are not cleanly separated. The pair supports the candidate's distinction between repeated operation and practice grammar. Similar sitting or effort can have different meaning, authority, and endpoint structures.

title: The Surplus Grain Test
unlike_statement: Unlike transfer theory, Hadot, Katz, Lindbeck, Pargament, upaya, VCE, and prior Lumenary ledgers, this idea says grain is only the predictive surplus of a prior dissolution target after rehearsed operation and receiving claimant grammar have already explained what they can.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Dogen-style practice-realization and inherited no-threshold practice cases.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence in universality. The model needs no-boundary, no-threshold, practice-realization, inherited-practice, and analyst-frame-refused values before public use.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If practice and realization are inseparable, with no boundary in realization and no beginning in practice, then formal transfer marker may be an analyst imposition. In inherited religious practice, there may also be no initial relinquishment event for the model to locate.

claim_units: Formal transfer markers identify instructions, permissions, and prohibitions that change agency grammar at a stage or threshold.
Phenomenological dissolution is uneven across view, affective conceit, habit, narrative identity, and social credential.
Sharp formal boundaries can coexist with long lived afterwork, as in shinjin or recognition of rigpa.
Gradual paths can still focus safeguards around agency grammar changes.
A usable comparative rubric should code function, marker, stage interval, claimant layer, support holder, credited source, forbidden claimant, gradient width, stalling point, safeguard, and repair.
The most dangerous interval is not initial relinquishment but the aftermath of partial dissolution, where the practitioner may own the surrender itself.
Traditions should show specific safeguard clusters around these vulnerable intervals rather than only generic humility warnings.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Psychotherapy, addiction recovery, and leadership transition.
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: High-risk reappropriation will cluster after the formal change, when the person starts owning being recovered, humble, transformed, delegated, or healed. Programs with explicit post-boundary repair should outperform programs that treat the formal change as the main event.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: A formal identity boundary, such as sobriety date, diagnosis acceptance, role transfer, or apology, can occur before psychological self-claiming releases across habit, affect, narrative, and social credential layers.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If relapse, status inflation, or identity rigidity is fully explained by habit strength, incentives, social support, clinical severity, and time since change, with no predictive gain from formal marker plus dissolution-gradient mismatch, the structure weakens.

exact_claim: Contemplative effort and gift should be coded as a two-track transfer profile: formal agency markers and phenomenological dissolution gradients. The highest risk comes after partial relinquishment, when remaining self-claiming reappropriates egolessness, surrender, recognition, or attainment.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Blind coders can identify agency markers from entry or stage passages, then predict warning clusters in separate manuals better than chance and better than generic humility themes.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Formal agency grammar changes predict where manuals place specific warnings and repairs.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Split-corpus manual coding across Theravada, Jodo Shinshu, Dzogchen or Mahamudra, Soto Zen, Christian grace, and Sufi fana or baqa sources.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Warnings distribute evenly, track only intensity or teacher authority, or cannot be predicted from formal markers.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Practitioner interviews distinguish view-release, affective conceit, habit, narrative identity, and social credential layers, and each layer predicts different forms of owning the insight.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Partial dissolution produces layer-specific reappropriation patterns.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Micro-phenomenological interviews with practitioners who report partial awakening, shinjin, direct recognition, surrender, or nondual insight.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Reports do not separate layers, or reappropriation is fully explained by ordinary pride, teacher feedback, status incentives, trauma, or community role.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Independent coders using each tradition's own vocabulary reach acceptable reliability on formal markers, forbidden claimant, support holder, and repair categories.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The rubric can be coded without importing Theravada categories into every tradition.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Inter-rater trial with primary-text excerpts and practitioner review panels.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Reliability fails, or coders converge only after replacing indigenous terms with Buddhist fetter or conceit language.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Students given stage-specific repair language show fewer ownership distortions than students given only general humility, safety, or effort instructions.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Repair instructions that explicitly address post-boundary reappropriation outperform generic anti-pride cautions.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Prospective training comparison in low-risk contemplative education settings.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Stage-specific repair adds no value after teacher relationship, screening, practice dose, group norms, and mental-health risk are controlled.

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as doctrine. Treat as a comparative and practitioner-facing coding heuristic.
Reduce the rubric to a testable core before public use: function, formal marker, claimant layer, support holder, safeguard, repair, analyst-frame refusal.
Use split-source blind coding: code agency markers from entry or stage texts, then predict safeguards from separate manuals or practitioner interviews.
Add mandatory values for no-boundary, no-threshold, inherited-practice, practice-realization, Other Power refusal, recognition-event, continuous authorization, and undecidable.
Run inter-rater reliability before using cross-tradition claims.

Ask practitioners for concrete failed-student cases and repair instructions, not agreement with the model.
Do not raise above extended unless the model predicts warning clusters or repairs better than prior Lumenary rubrics and established near-neighbor theories.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.61 to about 0.54. The two-track formulation is useful, but the primary-text close read shows that formal attainment versus residual self-claiming is already explicit in SN 22.89, Other Power boundary and anti-calculation repair are explicit in Shinran, and reappropriation is strongly anticipated by spiritual materialism. The remaining contribution is a comparative coding synthesis with testable warning-cluster predictions.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is standard fetter theory, Shinshu non-calculation, Dzogchen stabilization, spiritual materialism, upaya, or ordinary pastoral caution. They cannot name repairs beyond familiar warnings about pride, striving, doubt, role identity, or teacher dependence.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners independently identify formal thresholds and layer-specific reappropriation patterns, then report that the two-track rubric changes sequencing, student warnings, or repair language in ways not captured by existing models.

Does this rubric identify a repair that would not already come from spiritual materialism, upaya, religious coping, or ordinary teacher experience?
Are there cases in your tradition where the entire question of transfer, ownership, or agency grammar is misleading?

primary_pair: SN 22.89 Khemaka Sutta, Access to Insight, https://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/sn/sn22/sn22.089.than.html
